std::move(*listener_or_error);\\n594:   }\\n595: \\n596:   ListenerImpl& new_listener_ref = *new_listener;\\n597: \\n598:   bool added = false;\\n599:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\\n600:     ASSERT(workers_started_);\\n601:     new_listener->debugLog(\\\"update warming listener\\\");\\n602:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_warming_listener));\\n603:     // In this case we can just replace inline.\\n604:     *existing_warming_listener = std::move(new_listener);\\n605:   } else if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\\n606:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_active_listener));\\n607:     // In this case we have no warming listener, so what we do depends on whether workers\\n608:     // have been started or not.\\n609:     if (workers_started_) {\\n610:       new_listener->debugLog(\\\"add warming listener\\\");\\n611:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\\n612:     } else {\\n613:       new_listener->debugLog(\\\"update active listener\\\");\\n614:       *existing_active_listener = std::move(new_listener);\\n615:     }\\n616:   } else {\\n617:     // We have no warming or active listener so we need to make a new one. What we do depends on\\n618:     // whether workers have been started or not.\\n619:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(name, *new_listener));\\n620:     if (workers_started_) {\\n621:       new_listener->debugLog(\\\"add warming listener\\\");\\n622:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\\n623:     } else {\\n624:       new_listener->debugLog(\\\"add active listener\\\");\\n625:       active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\\n626:     }\\n627: \\n628:     added = true;\\n629:   }\\n630: \\n631:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\\n632:   if (added) {\\n633:     stats_.listener_added_.inc();\\n634:   } else {\\n635:     stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\\n636:   }\\n637: \\n638:   new_listener_ref.initialize();\\n639:   return true;\\n640: }\\n641: \\n642: bool ListenerManagerImpl::hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\\n643:                                                            const ListenerImpl& listener) {\\n644:   for (const auto& existing_listener : list) {\\n645:     if (existing_listener->hasDuplicatedAddress(listener)) {\\n646:       return true;\\n647:     }\\n648:   }\\n649:   return false;\\n650: }\\n651: \\n652: void ListenerManagerImpl::drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener) {\\n653:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\\n654:   std::list<DrainingListener>::iterator draining_it = draining_listeners_.emplace(\\n655:       draining_listeners_.begin(), std::move(listener), workers_.size());\\n656: \\n657:   // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\\n658:   // restart. Same below inside the lambda.\\n659:   stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\\n660: \\n661:   // Tell all workers to stop accepting new connections on this listener.\\n662:   draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\\\"draining listener\\\");\\n663:   const uint64_t listener_tag = draining_it->listener_->listenerTag();\\n664:   stopListener(*draining_it->listener_, {}, [this, listener_tag]() {\\n665:     for (auto& listener : draining_listeners_) {\\n666:       if (listener.listener_->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\\n667:         maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener.listener_);\\n668:       }\\n669:     }\\n670:   });\\n671: \\n672:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\\n673:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\\n674:   draining_it->listener_->localDrainManager().startDrainSequence([this, draining_it]() -> void {\\n675:     draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\\\"removing draining listener\\\");\\n676:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\\n677:       // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\\n678:       // to remove the listener.\\n679:       worker->removeListener(*draining_it->listener_, [this, draining_it]() -> void {\\n680:         // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to the\\n681:         // main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the listener\\n682:         // while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\\n683:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_it]() -> void {\\n684:           if (--draining_it->workers_pending_removal_ == 0) {\\n685:             draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\\\"draining listener removal complete\\\");\\n686:             draining_listeners_.erase(draining_it);\\n687:             stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\\n688:           }\\n689:         });\\n690:       });\\n691:     }\\n692:   });\\n693: \\n694:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\\n695: }\\n696: \\n697: ListenerManagerImpl::ListenerList::iterator\\n698: ListenerManagerImpl::getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name) {\\n699:   auto ret = listeners.end();\\n700:   for (auto it = listeners.begin(); it != listeners.end(); ++it) {\\n701:     if ((*it)->name() == name) {\\n702:       // There should only ever be a single listener per name in the list. We could return faster\\n703:       // but take the opportunity to assert that fact.\\n704:       ASSERT(ret == listeners.end());\\n705:       ret = it;\\n706:     }\\n707:   }\\n708:   return ret;\\n709: }\\n710: \\n711: std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\\n712: ListenerManagerImpl::listeners(ListenerState state) {\\n713:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>> ret;\\n714: \\n715:   size_t size = 0;\\n716:   size += state & WARMING ? warming_listeners_.size() : 0;\\n717:   size += state & ACTIVE ? active_listeners_.size() : 0;\\n718:   size += state & DRAINING ? draining_listeners_.size() : 0;\\n719:   ret.reserve(size);\\n720: \\n721:   if (state & WARMING) {\\n722:     for (const auto& listener : warming_listeners_) {\\n723:       ret.push_back(*listener);\\n724:     }\\n725:   }\\n726:   if (state & ACTIVE) {\\n727:     for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\\n728:       ret.push_back(*listener);\\n729:     }\\n730:   }\\n731:   if (state & DRAINING) {\\n732:     for (const auto& draining_listener : draining_listeners_) {\\n733:       ret.push_back(*(draining_listener.listener_));\\n734:     }\\n735:   }\\n736:   return ret;\\n737: }\\n738: \\n739: bool ListenerManagerImpl::doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener) {\\n740:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\\n741:     for (auto& socket_factory : listener.listenSocketFactories()) {\\n742:       absl::Status success = (socket_factory->doFinalPreWorkerInit());\\n743:       if (!success.ok()) {\\n744:         ENVOY_LOG(error, \\\"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\\\",\\n745:                   listener.name(), success.message());\\n746:         return false;\\n747:       }\\n748:     }\\n749:     return true;\\n750:   }\\n751:   END_TRY\\n752:   CATCH(EnvoyException & e, {\\n753:     ENVOY_LOG(error, \\\"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\\\", listener.name(),\\n754:               e.what());\\n755:     return false;\\n756:   });\\n757: }\\n758: \\n759: void ListenerManagerImpl::addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker,\\n760:                                               absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\\n761:                                               ListenerImpl& listener,\\n762:                                               ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback) {\\n763:   if (overridden_listener.has_value()) {\\n764:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"replacing existing listener {}\\\", overridden_listener.value());\\n765:   }\\n766:   worker.addListener(\\n767:       overridden_listener, listener,\\n768:       [this, completion_callback]() -> void {\\n769:         // The add listener completion runs on the worker thread. Post back to the main thread to\\n770:         // avoid locking.\\n771:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, completion_callback]() -> void {\\n772:           stats_.listener_create_success_.inc();\\n773:           if (completion_callback) {\\n774:             completion_callback();\\n775:           }\\n776:         });\\n777:       },\\n778:       server_.runtime(), server_.api().randomGenerator());\\n779: }\\n780: \\n781: void ListenerManagerImpl::onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener) {\\n782:   // The warmed listener should be added first so that the worker will accept new connections\\n783:   // when it stops listening on the old listener.\\n784:   if (!doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(listener)) {\\n785:     incListenerCreateFailureStat();\\n786:     // TODO(mattklein123): Technically we don't need to remove the active listener if one exists.\\n787:     // The following call will remove both.\\n788:     removeListenerInternal(listener.name(), true);\\n789:     return;\\n790:   }\\n791:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\\n792:     addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, listener, nullptr);\\n793:   }\\n794: \\n795:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\\n796:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\\n797: \\n798:   (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\\\"warm complete. updating active listener\\\");\\n799:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\\n800:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\\n801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0@75cca99ee9fea5a6b699af6a2124ec7404fe5d13/-/blob/source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.cc%3FL500-800\"}",

This check needs to be done against both\n567:   // warming and active.\n568:   if ((existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end() &&\n569:        (*existing_warming_listener)->blockUpdate(hash)) ||\n570:       (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n571:        (*existing_active_listener)->blockUpdate(hash))) {\n572:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"duplicate/locked listener '{}'. no add/update\", name);\n573:     return false;\n574:   }\n575: \n576:   ListenerImplPtr new_listener = nullptr;\n577: \n578:   // In place filter chain update depends on the active listener at worker.\n579:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n580:       (*existing_active_listener)->supportUpdateFilterChain(config, workers_started_)) {\n581:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"use in place update filter chain update path for listener name={} hash={}\",\n582:               name, hash);\n583:     auto listener_or_error =\n584:         (*existing_active_listener)->newListenerWithFilterChain(config, workers_started_, hash);\n585:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n586:     new_listener = std::move(*listener_or_error);\n587:     stats_.listener_in_place_updated_.inc();\n588:   } else {\n589:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"use full listener update path for listener name={} hash={}\", name, hash);\n590:     auto listener_or_error = ListenerImpl::create(config, version_info, *this, name, added_via_api,\n591:                                                   workers_started_, hash);\n592:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n593:     new_listener = std::move(*listener_or_error);\n594:   }\n595: \n596:   ListenerImpl& new_listener_ref = *new_listener;\n597: \n598:   bool added = false;\n599:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n600:     ASSERT(workers_started_);\n601:     new_listener->debugLog(\"update warming listener\");\n602:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_warming_listener));\n603:     // In this case we can just replace inline.\n604:     *existing_warming_listener = std::move(new_listener);\n605:   } else if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n606:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_active_listener));\n607:     // In this case we have no warming listener, so what we do depends on whether workers\n608:     // have been started or not.\n609:     if (workers_started_) {\n610:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add warming listener\");\n611:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n612:     } else {\n613:       new_listener->debugLog(\"update active listener\");\n614:       *existing_active_listener = std::move(new_listener);\n615:     }\n616:   } else {\n617:     // We have no warming or active listener so we need to make a new one. What we do depends on\n618:     // whether workers have been started or not.\n619:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(name, *new_listener));\n620:     if (workers_started_) {\n621:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add warming listener\");\n622:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n623:     } else {\n624:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add active listener\");\n625:       active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n626:     }\n627: \n628:     added = true;\n629:   }\n630: \n631:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n632:   if (added) {\n633:     stats_.listener_added_.inc();\n634:   } else {\n635:     stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\n636:   }\n637: \n638:   new_listener_ref.initialize();\n639:   return true;\n640: }\n641: \n642: bool ListenerManagerImpl::hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\n643:                                                            const ListenerImpl& listener) {\n644:   for (const auto& existing_listener : list) {\n645:     if (existing_listener->hasDuplicatedAddress(listener)) {\n646:       return true;\n647:     }\n648:   }\n649:   return false;\n650: }\n651: \n652: void ListenerManagerImpl::drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener) {\n653:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\n654:   std::list<DrainingListener>::iterator draining_it = draining_listeners_.emplace(\n655:       draining_listeners_.begin(), std::move(listener), workers_.size());\n656: \n657:   // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\n658:   // restart. Same below inside the lambda.\n659:   stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n660: \n661:   // Tell all workers to stop accepting new connections on this listener.\n662:   draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener\");\n663:   const uint64_t listener_tag = draining_it->listener_->listenerTag();\n664:   stopListener(*draining_it->listener_, {}, [this, listener_tag]() {\n665:     for (auto& listener : draining_listeners_) {\n666:       if (listener.listener_->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\n667:         maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener.listener_);\n668:       }\n669:     }\n670:   });\n671: \n672:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n673:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n674:   draining_it->listener_->localDrainManager().startDrainSequence([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n675:     draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"removing draining listener\");\n676:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n677:       // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n678:       // to remove the listener.\n679:       worker->removeListener(*draining_it->listener_, [this, draining_it]() -> void {\n680:         // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to the\n681:         // main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the listener\n682:         // while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n683:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n684:           if (--draining_it->workers_pending_removal_ == 0) {\n685:             draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener removal complete\");\n686:             draining_listeners_.erase(draining_it);\n687:             stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n688:           }\n689:         });\n690:       });\n691:     }\n692:   });\n693: \n694:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n695: }\n696: \n697: ListenerManagerImpl::ListenerList::iterator\n698: ListenerManagerImpl::getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name) {\n699:   auto ret = listeners.end();\n700:   for (auto it = listeners.begin(); it != listeners.end(); ++it) {\n701:     if ((*it)->name() == name) {\n702:       // There should only ever be a single listener per name in the list. We could return faster\n703:       // but take the opportunity to assert that fact.\n704:       ASSERT(ret == listeners.end());\n705:       ret = it;\n706:     }\n707:   }\n708:   return ret;\n709: }\n710: \n711: std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n712: ListenerManagerImpl::listeners(ListenerState state) {\n713:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>> ret;\n714: \n715:   size_t size = 0;\n716:   size += state & WARMING ? warming_listeners_.size() : 0;\n717:   size += state & ACTIVE ? active_listeners_.size() : 0;\n718:   size += state & DRAINING ? draining_listeners_.size() : 0;\n719:   ret.reserve(size);\n720: \n721:   if (state & WARMING) {\n722:     for (const auto& listener : warming_listeners_) {\n723:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n724:     }\n725:   }\n726:   if (state & ACTIVE) {\n727:     for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n728:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n729:     }\n730:   }\n731:   if (state & DRAINING) {\n732:     for (const auto& draining_listener : draining_listeners_) {\n733:       ret.push_back(*(draining_listener.listener_));\n734:     }\n735:   }\n736:   return ret;\n737: }\n738: \n739: bool ListenerManagerImpl::doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n740:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n741:     for (auto& socket_factory : listener.listenSocketFactories()) {\n742:       absl::Status success = (socket_factory->doFinalPreWorkerInit());\n743:       if (!success.ok()) {\n744:         ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\",\n745:                   listener.name(), success.message());\n746:         return false;\n747:       }\n748:     }\n749:     return true;\n750:   }\n751:   END_TRY\n752:   CATCH(EnvoyException & e, {\n753:     ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\", listener.name(),\n754:               e.what());\n755:     return false;\n756:   });\n757: }\n758: \n759: void ListenerManagerImpl::addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker,\n760:                                               absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\n761:                                               ListenerImpl& listener,\n762:                                               ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback) {\n763:   if (overridden_listener.has_value()) {\n764:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"replacing existing listener {}\", overridden_listener.value());\n765:   }\n766:   worker.addListener(\n767:       overridden_listener, listener,\n768:       [this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n769:         // The add listener completion runs on the worker thread. Post back to the main thread to\n770:         // avoid locking.\n771:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n772:           stats_.listener_create_success_.inc();\n773:           if (completion_callback) {\n774:             completion_callback();\n775:           }\n776:         });\n777:       },\n778:       server_.runtime(), server_.api().randomGenerator());\n779: }\n780: \n781: void ListenerManagerImpl::onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n782:   // The warmed listener should be added first so that the worker will accept new connections\n783:   // when it stops listening on the old listener.\n784:   if (!doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(listener)) {\n785:     incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n786:     // TODO(mattklein123): Technically we don't need to remove the active listener if one exists.\n787:     // The following call will remove both.\n788:     removeListenerInternal(listener.name(), true);\n789:     return;\n790:   }\n791:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n792:     addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, listener, nullptr);\n793:   }\n794: \n795:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n796:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n797: \n798:   (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"warm complete. updating active listener\");\n799:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n800:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n801: ",

#define ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(COUNTER, GAUGE)                                                 \\\\\\n144:   COUNTER(listener_added) \\n157: /**\\n158:  * Struct definition for all listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\\n159:  */\\n160: struct ListenerManagerStats {\\n161:   ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT, GENERATE_GAUGE_STRUCT)\\n162: };\\n163: \\n164: /**\\n165:  * Provides the draining filter chains and the functionality to schedule listener destroy.\\n166:  */\\n167: class DrainingFilterChainsManager {\\n168: public:\\n169:   DrainingFilterChainsManager(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\\n170:                               uint64_t workers_pending_removal);\\n171:   uint64_t getDrainingListenerTag() const { return draining_listener_->listenerTag(); }\\n172:   const std::list<const Network::FilterChain*>& getDrainingFilterChains() const {\\n173:     return draining_filter_chains_;\\n174:   }\\n175:   ListenerImpl& getDrainingListener() const { return *draining_listener_; }\\n176:   uint64_t decWorkersPendingRemoval() { return --workers_pending_removal_; }\\n177: \\n178:   // Schedule listener destroy.\\n179:   void startDrainSequence(std::chrono::seconds drain_time, Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\\n180:                           std::function<void()> completion) {\\n181:     drain_sequence_completion_ = completion;\\n182:     ASSERT(!drain_timer_);\\n183: \\n184:     drain_timer_ = dispatcher.createTimer([this]() -> void { drain_sequence_completion_(); });\\n185:     drain_timer_->enableTimer(drain_time);\\n186:   }\\n187: \\n188:   void addFilterChainToDrain(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain) {\\n189:     draining_filter_chains_.push_back(&filter_chain);\\n190:   }\\n191: \\n192:   uint32_t numDrainingFilterChains() const { return draining_filter_chains_.size(); }\\n193: \\n194: private:\\n195:   ListenerImplPtr draining_listener_;\\n196:   std::list<const Network::FilterChain*> draining_filter_chains_;\\n197: \\n198:   uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\\n199:   Event::TimerPtr drain_timer_;\\n200:   std::function<void()> drain_sequence_completion_;\\n201: };\\n202: \\n203: /**\\n204:  * Implementation of ListenerManager.\\n205:  */\\n206: class ListenerManagerImpl : public ListenerManager, Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\\n207: public:\\n208:   ListenerManagerImpl(Instance& server, std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\\n209:  void inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n214: \\n215:   // Server::ListenerManager\\n216:   absl::StatusOr<bool> addOrUpdateListener(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n217:                                            const std::string& version_info,\\n218:                                            bool added_via_api) override;\\n219:   void createLdsApi(const envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource& lds_config,\\n220:                     const xds::core::v3::ResourceLocator* lds_resources_locator) override {\\n221:     ASSERT(lds_api_ == nullptr);\\n222:     lds_api_ = factory_->createLdsApi(lds_config, lds_resources_locator);\\n223:   }\\n224:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\\n225:   listeners(ListenerState state = ListenerState::ACTIVE) override;\\n226:   uint64_t numConnections() const override;\\n227:   bool removeListener(const std::string& listener_name) override;\\n228:   absl::Status startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog, std::function<void()> callback) override;\\n229:   void stopListeners(StopListenersType stop_listeners_type,\\n230:                      const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) override;\\n231:   void stopWorkers() override;\\n232:   void beginListenerUpdate() override { error_state_tracker_.clear(); }\\n233:   void endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_state) override;\\n234:   bool isWorkerStarted() override { return workers_started_; }\\n235:   Http::Context& httpContext() { return server_.httpContext(); }\\n236:   ApiListenerOptRef apiListener() override;\\n237: \\n238:   Quic::QuicStatNames& quicStatNames() { return quic_stat_names_; }\\n239: \\n240:   Instance& server_;\\n241:   std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory> factory_;\\n242: \\n243: private:\\n244:   using ListenerList = std::list<ListenerImplPtr>;\\n245:   /**\\n246:    * Callback invoked when a listener initialization is completed on worker.\\n247:    */\\n248:   using ListenerCompletionCallback = std::function<void()>;\\n249: \\n250:   absl::StatusOr<bool>\\n251:   addOrUpdateListenerInternal(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n252:                               const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api,\\n253:                               const std::string& name);\\n254:   bool removeListenerInternal(const std::string& listener_name, bool dynamic_listeners_only);\\n255: \\n256:   struct DrainingListener {\\n257:     DrainingListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener, uint64_t workers_pending_removal)\\n258:         : listener_(std::move(listener)), workers_pending_removal_(workers_pending_removal) {}\\n259: \\n260:     ListenerImplPtr listener_;\\n261:     uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\\n262:   };\\n263: \\n264:   bool doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n265:   void addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker, absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\\n266:                            ListenerImpl& listener, ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback);\\n267: \\n268:   ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr dumpListenerConfigs(const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher);\\n269:   static ListenerManagerStats generateStats(Stats::Scope& scope);\\n270:   static bool hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\\n271:                                                const ListenerImpl& listener);\\n272:   void updateWarmingActiveGauges() {\\n273:     // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\\n274:     // restart.\\n275:     stats_.total_listeners_warming_.set(warming_listeners_.size());\\n276:     stats_.total_listeners_active_.set(active_listeners_.size());\\n277:   }\\n278:   bool listenersStopped(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\\n279:     // Currently all listeners in a given direction are stopped because of the way admin\\n280:     // drain_listener functionality is implemented. This needs to be revisited, if that changes - if\\n281:     // we support drain by listener name,for example.\\n282:     return stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::All ||\\n283:            (stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::InboundOnly &&\\n284:             config.traffic_direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND);\\n285:   }\\n286:   void incListenerCreateFailureStat() {\\n287:     // TODO(mattklein123): Now that we no longer create sockets and apply options on workers,\\n288:     // this stat is confusingly named and should be more specific, however it's unclear if it's\\n289:     // worth changing.\\n290:     stats_.listener_create_failure_.inc();\\n291:   }\\n292: \\n293:   /**\\n294:    * Mark a listener for draining. The listener will no longer be considered active but will remain\\n295:    * present to allow connection draining.\\n296:    * @param listener supplies the listener to drain.\\n297:    */\\n298:   void drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener);\\n299: \\n300:   /**\\n301:    * Start to draining filter chains that are owned by draining listener but not owned by\\n302:    * new_listener. The new listener should have taken over the listener socket and partial of the\\n303:    * filter chains from listener. This method is used by in place filter chain update.\\n304:    * @param draining_listener supplies the listener to be replaced.\\n305:    * @param new_listener supplies the new listener config which is going to replace the draining\\n306:    * listener.\\n307:    */\\n308:   void drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener, ListenerImpl& new_listener);\\n309: \\n310:   /**\\n311:    * Stop a listener. The listener will stop accepting new connections and its socket will be\\n312:    * closed.\\n313:    * @param listener supplies the listener to stop.\\n314:    * @param options additional options to be passed through to shutdownListener.\\n315:    * @param completion supplies the completion to be called when all workers are stopped accepting\\n316:    * new connections. This completion is called on the main thread.\\n317:    */\\n318:   void stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\\n319:                     const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\\n320:                     std::function<void()> completion);\\n321: \\n322:   /**\\n323:    * Get a listener by name. This routine is used because listeners have inherent order in static\\n324:    * configuration and especially for tests. Thus, we can't use a map.\\n325:    * @param listeners supplies the listener list to look in.\\n326:    * @param name supplies the name to search for.\\n327:    */\\n328:   ListenerList::iterator getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name);\\n329: \\n330:   absl::Status setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name, ListenerImpl& listener);\\n331:   absl::Status createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n332: \\n333:   void maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n334:   absl::Status setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\\n335:                                              const ListenerImpl& existing_listener);\\n336: \\n337:   ApiListenerPtr api_listener_;\\n338:   // Active listeners are listeners that are currently accepting new connections on the workers.\\n339:   ListenerList active_listeners_;\\n340:   // Warming listeners are listeners that may need further initialization via the listener's init\\n341:   // manager. For example, RDS, or in the future KDS. Once a listener is done warming it will\\n342:   // be transitioned to active.\\n343:   ListenerList warming_listeners_;\\n344:   // Draining listeners are listeners that are in the process of being drained and removed. They\\n345:   // go through two phases where first the workers stop accepting new connections and existing\\n346:   // connections are drained. Then after that time period the listener is removed from all workers\\n347:   // and any remaining connections are closed.\\n348:   std::list<DrainingListener> draining_listeners_;\\n349:   std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager> draining_filter_chains_manager_;\\n350: \\n351:   std::vector<WorkerPtr> workers_;\\n352:   bool workers_started_{};\\n353:   absl::optional<StopListenersType> stop_listeners_type_;\\n354:   Stats::ScopeSharedPtr scope_;\\n355:   ListenerManagerStats stats_;\\n356:   ConfigTracker::EntryOwnerPtr config_tracker_entry_;\\n357:   LdsApiPtr lds_api_;\\n358:   const bool enable_dispatcher_stats_{};\\n359:   using UpdateFailureState = envoy::admin::v3::UpdateFailureState;\\n360:   absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, std::unique_ptr<UpdateFailureState>> error_state_tracker_;\\n361:   FailureStates overall_error_state_;\\n362:   Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names_;\\n363: };\\n364: \\n365: class ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder : public FilterChainFactoryBuilder {\\n366: public:\\n367:   ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder(\\n368:       ListenerImpl& listener, Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context);\\n369: \\n370:   absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\\n371:   buildFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\\n372:                    FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) const override;\\n373: \\n374: private:\\n375:   absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr> buildFilterChainInternal(\\n376:       const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\\n377:       Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContextPtr&& filter_chain_factory_context) const;\\n378: \\n379:   ListenerImpl& listener_;\\n380:   ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validator_;\\n381:   ListenerComponentFactory& listener_component_factory_;\\n382:   Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context_;\\n383: };\\n384: \\n385: class DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl : public ListenerManagerFactory {\\n386: public:\\n387:   std::unique_ptr<ListenerManager>\\n388:   createListenerManager(Instance& server, std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\\n389:                         WorkerFactory& worker_factory, bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\\n390:                         Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names) override {\\n391:     return std::make_unique<ListenerManagerImpl>(server, std::move(factory), worker_factory,\\n392:                                                  enable_dispatcher_stats, quic_stat_names);\\n393:   }\\n394:   std::string name() const override {\\n395:     return Config::ServerExtensionValues::get().DEFAULT_LISTENER;\\n396:   }\\n397:   ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr createEmptyConfigProto() override {\\n398:     return std::make_unique<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerManager>();\\n399:   }\\n400: };\\n401: \\n402: DECLARE_FACTORY(DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl);\\n403: \\n404: } // namespace Server\\n405: } // namespace Envoy\\n406: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0@75cca99ee9fea5a6b699af6a2124ec7404fe5d13/-/blob/source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.h\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: #pragma once\n2: \n3: #include <memory>\n4: \n5: #include \"envoy/admin/v3/config_dump.pb.h\"\n6: #include \"envoy/config/core/v3/address.pb.h\"\n7: #include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n8: #include \"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.pb.h\"\n9: #include \"envoy/config/listener/v3/listener.pb.h\"\n10: #include \"envoy/config/listener/v3/listener_components.pb.h\"\n11: #include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n12: #include \"envoy/network/listen_socket.h\"\n13: #include \"envoy/server/api_listener.h\"\n14: #include \"envoy/server/filter_config.h\"\n15: #include \"envoy/server/instance.h\"\n16: #include \"envoy/server/listener_manager.h\"\n17: #include \"envoy/server/transport_socket_config.h\"\n18: #include \"envoy/server/worker.h\"\n19: #include \"envoy/stats/scope.h\"\n20: \n21: #include \"source/common/config/well_known_names.h\"\n22: #include \"source/common/filter/config_discovery_impl.h\"\n23: #include \"source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_factory_context_callback.h\"\n24: #include \"source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h\"\n25: #include \"source/common/listener_manager/lds_api.h\"\n26: #include \"source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.h\"\n27: #include \"source/common/quic/quic_stat_names.h\"\n28: #include \"source/server/listener_manager_factory.h\"\n29: \n30: namespace Envoy {\n31: namespace Server {\n32: \n33: namespace Configuration {\n34: class TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl;\n35: }\n36: \n37: class ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder;\n38: \n39: /**\n40:  * Prod implementation of ListenerComponentFactory that creates real sockets and attempts to fetch\n41:  * sockets from the parent process via the hot restarter. The filter factory list is created from\n42:  * statically registered filters.\n43:  */\n44: class ProdListenerComponentFactory : public ListenerComponentFactory,\n45:                                      Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n46: public:\n47:   ProdListenerComponentFactory(Instance& server) : server_(server) {}\n48:   /**\n49:    * Static worker for createNetworkFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n50:    */\n51:   static absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList> createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(\n52:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\n53:       Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context,\n54:       Filter::NetworkFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n55: \n56:   /**\n57:    * Static worker for createListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n58:    */\n59:   static absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n60:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n61:       Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context,\n62:       Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n63: \n64:   /**\n65:    * Static worker for createUdpListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n66:    */\n67:   static absl::StatusOr<std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb>>\n68:   createUdpListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n69:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n70:       Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context);\n71: \n72:   /**\n73:    * Static worker for createQuicListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n74:    */\n75:   static absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList>\n76:   createQuicListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n77:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n78:       Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context,\n79:       Filter::QuicListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n80: \n81:   static Network::ListenerFilterMatcherSharedPtr\n82:   createListenerFilterMatcher(const envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter& listener_filter);\n83: \n84:   // Server::ListenerComponentFactory\n85:   LdsApiPtr createLdsApi(const envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource& lds_config,\n86:                          const xds::core::v3::ResourceLocator* lds_resources_locator) override {\n87:     return std::make_unique<LdsApiImpl>(\n88:         lds_config, lds_resources_locator, server_.clusterManager(), server_.initManager(),\n89:         *server_.stats().rootScope(), server_.listenerManager(),\n90:         server_.messageValidationContext().dynamicValidationVisitor());\n91:   }\n92:   absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList> createNetworkFilterFactoryList(\n93:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\n94:       Server::Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context) override {\n95:     return createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, filter_chain_factory_context,\n96:                                               network_config_provider_manager_);\n97:   }\n98:   absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryList(\n99:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n100:       Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n101:     return createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context,\n102:                                                tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_);\n103:   }\n104:   absl::StatusOr<std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb>>\n105:   createUdpListenerFilterFactoryList(\n106:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n107:       Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n108:     return createUdpListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context);\n109:   }\n110:   absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList> createQuicListenerFilterFactoryList(\n111:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n112:       Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n113:     return createQuicListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context,\n114:                                                    quic_listener_config_provider_manager_);\n115:   }\n116:   absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> createListenSocket(\n117:       Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address, Network::Socket::Type socket_type,\n118:       const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options, BindType bind_type,\n119:       const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t worker_index) override;\n120: \n121:   DrainManagerPtr\n122:   createDrainManager(envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::DrainType drain_type) override;\n123:   uint64_t nextListenerTag() override { return next_listener_tag_++; }\n124:   Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl*\n125:   getTcpListenerConfigProviderManager() override {\n126:     return &tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_;\n127:   }\n128: \n129: private:\n130:   Instance& server_;\n131:   uint64_t next_listener_tag_{1};\n132:   Filter::NetworkFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl network_config_provider_manager_;\n133:   Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_;\n134:   Filter::QuicListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl quic_listener_config_provider_manager_;\n135: };\n136: \n137: class ListenerImpl;\n138: using ListenerImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>;\n139: \n140: /**\n141:  * All listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\n142:  */\n143: #define ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(COUNTER, GAUGE)                                                 \\\n144:   COUNTER(listener_added)                                                                          \\\n145:   COUNTER(listener_create_failure)                                                                 \\\n146:   COUNTER(listener_create_success)                                                                 \\\n147:   COUNTER(listener_in_place_updated)                                                               \\\n148:   COUNTER(listener_modified)                                                                       \\\n149:   COUNTER(listener_removed)                                                                        \\\n150:   COUNTER(listener_stopped)                                                                        \\\n151:   GAUGE(total_filter_chains_draining, NeverImport)                                                 \\\n152:   GAUGE(total_listeners_active, NeverImport)                                                       \\\n153:   GAUGE(total_listeners_draining, NeverImport)                                                     \\\n154:   GAUGE(total_listeners_warming, NeverImport)                                                      \\\n155:   GAUGE(workers_started, NeverImport)\n156: \n157: /**\n158:  * Struct definition for all listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\n159:  */\n160: struct ListenerManagerStats {\n161:   ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT, GENERATE_GAUGE_STRUCT)\n162: };\n163: \n164: /**\n165:  * Provides the draining filter chains and the functionality to schedule listener destroy.\n166:  */\n167: class DrainingFilterChainsManager {\n168: public:\n169:   DrainingFilterChainsManager(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n170:                               uint64_t workers_pending_removal);\n171:   uint64_t getDrainingListenerTag() const { return draining_listener_->listenerTag(); }\n172:   const std::list<const Network::FilterChain*>& getDrainingFilterChains() const {\n173:     return draining_filter_chains_;\n174:   }\n175:   ListenerImpl& getDrainingListener() const { return *draining_listener_; }\n176:   uint64_t decWorkersPendingRemoval() { return --workers_pending_removal_; }\n177: \n178:   // Schedule listener destroy.\n179:   void startDrainSequence(std::chrono::seconds drain_time, Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\n180:                           std::function<void()> completion) {\n181:     drain_sequence_completion_ = completion;\n182:     ASSERT(!drain_timer_);\n183: \n184:     drain_timer_ = dispatcher.createTimer([this]() -> void { drain_sequence_completion_(); });\n185:     drain_timer_->enableTimer(drain_time);\n186:   }\n187: \n188:   void addFilterChainToDrain(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain) {\n189:     draining_filter_chains_.push_back(&filter_chain);\n190:   }\n191: \n192:   uint32_t numDrainingFilterChains() const { return draining_filter_chains_.size(); }\n193: \n194: private:\n195:   ListenerImplPtr draining_listener_;\n196:   std::list<const Network::FilterChain*> draining_filter_chains_;\n197: \n198:   uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\n199:   Event::TimerPtr drain_timer_;\n200:   std::function<void()> drain_sequence_completion_;\n201: };\n202: \n203: /**\n204:  * Implementation of ListenerManager.\n205:  */\n206: class ListenerManagerImpl : public ListenerManager, Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n207: public:\n208:   ListenerManagerImpl(Instance& server, std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\n209:                       WorkerFactory& worker_factory, bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\n210:                       Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names);\n211: \n212:   void onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener);\n213:   void inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener);\n214: \n215:   // Server::ListenerManager\n216:   absl::StatusOr<bool> addOrUpdateListener(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n217:                                            const std::string& version_info,\n218:                                            bool added_via_api) override;\n219:   void createLdsApi(const envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource& lds_config,\n220:                     const xds::core::v3::ResourceLocator* lds_resources_locator) override {\n221:     ASSERT(lds_api_ == nullptr);\n222:     lds_api_ = factory_->createLdsApi(lds_config, lds_resources_locator);\n223:   }\n224:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n225:   listeners(ListenerState state = ListenerState::ACTIVE) override;\n226:   uint64_t numConnections() const override;\n227:   bool removeListener(const std::string& listener_name) override;\n228:   absl::Status startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog, std::function<void()> callback) override;\n229:   void stopListeners(StopListenersType stop_listeners_type,\n230:                      const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) override;\n231:   void stopWorkers() override;\n232:   void beginListenerUpdate() override { error_state_tracker_.clear(); }\n233:   void endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_state) override;\n234:   bool isWorkerStarted() override { return workers_started_; }\n235:   Http::Context& httpContext() { return server_.httpContext(); }\n236:   ApiListenerOptRef apiListener() override;\n237: \n238:   Quic::QuicStatNames& quicStatNames() { return quic_stat_names_; }\n239: \n240:   Instance& server_;\n241:   std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory> factory_;\n242: \n243: private:\n244:   using ListenerList = std::list<ListenerImplPtr>;\n245:   /**\n246:    * Callback invoked when a listener initialization is completed on worker.\n247:    */\n248:   using ListenerCompletionCallback = std::function<void()>;\n249: \n250:   absl::StatusOr<bool>\n251:   addOrUpdateListenerInternal(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n252:                               const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api,\n253:                               const std::string& name);\n254:   bool removeListenerInternal(const std::string& listener_name, bool dynamic_listeners_only);\n255: \n256:   struct DrainingListener {\n257:     DrainingListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener, uint64_t workers_pending_removal)\n258:         : listener_(std::move(listener)), workers_pending_removal_(workers_pending_removal) {}\n259: \n260:     ListenerImplPtr listener_;\n261:     uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\n262:   };\n263: \n264:   bool doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener);\n265:   void addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker, absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\n266:                            ListenerImpl& listener, ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback);\n267: \n268:   ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr dumpListenerConfigs(const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher);\n269:   static ListenerManagerStats generateStats(Stats::Scope& scope);\n270:   static bool hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\n271:                                                const ListenerImpl& listener);\n272:   void updateWarmingActiveGauges() {\n273:     // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\n274:     // restart.\n275:     stats_.total_listeners_warming_.set(warming_listeners_.size());\n276:     stats_.total_listeners_active_.set(active_listeners_.size());\n277:   }\n278:   bool listenersStopped(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n279:     // Currently all listeners in a given direction are stopped because of the way admin\n280:     // drain_listener functionality is implemented. This needs to be revisited, if that changes - if\n281:     // we support drain by listener name,for example.\n282:     return stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::All ||\n283:            (stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::InboundOnly &&\n284:             config.traffic_direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND);\n285:   }\n286:   void incListenerCreateFailureStat() {\n287:     // TODO(mattklein123): Now that we no longer create sockets and apply options on workers,\n288:     // this stat is confusingly named and should be more specific, however it's unclear if it's\n289:     // worth changing.\n290:     stats_.listener_create_failure_.inc();\n291:   }\n292: \n293:   /**\n294:    * Mark a listener for draining. The listener will no longer be considered active but will remain\n295:    * present to allow connection draining.\n296:    * @param listener supplies the listener to drain.\n297:    */\n298:   void drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener);\n299: \n300:   /**\n301:    * Start to draining filter chains that are owned by draining listener but not owned by\n302:    * new_listener. The new listener should have taken over the listener socket and partial of the\n303:    * filter chains from listener. This method is used by in place filter chain update.\n304:    * @param draining_listener supplies the listener to be replaced.\n305:    * @param new_listener supplies the new listener config which is going to replace the draining\n306:    * listener.\n307:    */\n308:   void drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener, ListenerImpl& new_listener);\n309: \n310:   /**\n311:    * Stop a listener. The listener will stop accepting new connections and its socket will be\n312:    * closed.\n313:    * @param listener supplies the listener to stop.\n314:    * @param options additional options to be passed through to shutdownListener.\n315:    * @param completion supplies the completion to be called when all workers are stopped accepting\n316:    * new connections. This completion is called on the main thread.\n317:    */\n318:   void stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n319:                     const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n320:                     std::function<void()> completion);\n321: \n322:   /**\n323:    * Get a listener by name. This routine is used because listeners have inherent order in static\n324:    * configuration and especially for tests. Thus, we can't use a map.\n325:    * @param listeners supplies the listener list to look in.\n326:    * @param name supplies the name to search for.\n327:    */\n328:   ListenerList::iterator getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name);\n329: \n330:   absl::Status setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name, ListenerImpl& listener);\n331:   absl::Status createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener);\n332: \n333:   void maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener);\n334:   absl::Status setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\n335:                                              const ListenerImpl& existing_listener);\n336: \n337:   ApiListenerPtr api_listener_;\n338:   // Active listeners are listeners that are currently accepting new connections on the workers.\n339:   ListenerList active_listeners_;\n340:   // Warming listeners are listeners that may need further initialization via the listener's init\n341:   // manager. For example, RDS, or in the future KDS. Once a listener is done warming it will\n342:   // be transitioned to active.\n343:   ListenerList warming_listeners_;\n344:   // Draining listeners are listeners that are in the process of being drained and removed. They\n345:   // go through two phases where first the workers stop accepting new connections and existing\n346:   // connections are drained. Then after that time period the listener is removed from all workers\n347:   // and any remaining connections are closed.\n348:   std::list<DrainingListener> draining_listeners_;\n349:   std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager> draining_filter_chains_manager_;\n350: \n351:   std::vector<WorkerPtr> workers_;\n352:   bool workers_started_{};\n353:   absl::optional<StopListenersType> stop_listeners_type_;\n354:   Stats::ScopeSharedPtr scope_;\n355:   ListenerManagerStats stats_;\n356:   ConfigTracker::EntryOwnerPtr config_tracker_entry_;\n357:   LdsApiPtr lds_api_;\n358:   const bool enable_dispatcher_stats_{};\n359:   using UpdateFailureState = envoy::admin::v3::UpdateFailureState;\n360:   absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, std::unique_ptr<UpdateFailureState>> error_state_tracker_;\n361:   FailureStates overall_error_state_;\n362:   Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names_;\n363: };\n364: \n365: class ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder : public FilterChainFactoryBuilder {\n366: public:\n367:   ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder(\n368:       ListenerImpl& listener, Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context);\n369: \n370:   absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\n371:   buildFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n372:                    FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) const override;\n373: \n374: private:\n375:   absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr> buildFilterChainInternal(\n376:       const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n377:       Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContextPtr&& filter_chain_factory_context) const;\n378: \n379:   ListenerImpl& listener_;\n380:   ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validator_;\n381:   ListenerComponentFactory& listener_component_factory_;\n382:   Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context_;\n383: };\n384: \n385: class DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl : public ListenerManagerFactory {\n386: public:\n387:   std::unique_ptr<ListenerManager>\n388:   createListenerManager(Instance& server, std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\n389:                         WorkerFactory& worker_factory, bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\n390:                         Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names) override {\n391:     return std::make_unique<ListenerManagerImpl>(server, std::move(factory), worker_factory,\n392:                                                  enable_dispatcher_stats, quic_stat_names);\n393:   }\n394:   std::string name() const override {\n395:     return Config::ServerExtensionValues::get().DEFAULT_LISTENER;\n396:   }\n397:   ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr createEmptyConfigProto() override {\n398:     return std::make_unique<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerManager>();\n399:   }\n400: };\n401: \n402: DECLARE_FACTORY(DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl);\n403: \n404: } // namespace Server\n405: } // namespace Envoy\n406: ",

Once a socket is created, its port\\n107:   // will be set to the binding port.\\n108:   Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr local_address_;\\n109:   const Network::Socket::Type socket_type_;\\n110:   const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr options_;\\n111:   const std::string listener_name_;\\n112:   const uint32_t tcp_backlog_size_;\\n113:   ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type_;\\n114:   const Network::SocketCreationOptions socket_creation_options_;\\n115:   // One socket for each worker, pre-created before the workers fetch the sockets. There are\\n116:   // 3 different cases:\\n117:   // 1) All are null when doing config validation.\\n118:   // 2) A single socket has been duplicated for each worker (no reuse_port).\\n119:   // 3) A unique socket for each worker (reuse_port).\\n120:   //\\n121:   // TODO(mattklein123): If a listener does not bind, it still has a socket. This is confusing\\n122:   // and not needed and can be cleaned up.\\n123:   std::vector<Network::SocketSharedPtr> sockets_;\\n124: };\\n125: \\n126: // TODO(mattklein123): Consider getting rid of pre-worker start and post-worker start code by\\n127: //                     initializing all listeners after workers are started.\\n128: \\n129: class ListenerImpl;\\n130: \\n131: /**\\n132:  * The common functionality shared by PerListenerFilterFactoryContexts and\\n133:  * PerFilterChainFactoryFactoryContexts.\\n134:  */\\n135: class ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl final : public Server::FactoryContextImplBase,\\n136:                                              public Network::DrainDecision {\\n137: public:\\n138:   ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl(Envoy::Server::Instance& server,\\n139:                                  ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\\n140:                                  const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n141:                                  Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager);\\n142: \\n143:   Init::Manager& initManager() override;\\n144:   Network::DrainDecision& drainDecision() override;\\n145: \\n146:   // DrainDecision\\n147:   bool drainClose() const override {\\n148:     return drain_manager_->drainClose() || server_.drainManager().drainClose();\\n149:   }\\n150:   Server::DrainManager& drainManager();\\n151:   friend class ListenerImpl;\\n152: \\n153: private:\\n154:   const Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager_;\\n155: };\\n156: \\n157: // TODO(lambdai): Strip the interface since ListenerFactoryContext only need to support\\n158: // ListenerFilterChain creation. e.g, Is listenerMetaData() required? }\\n185:   friend class ListenerImpl;\\n186: \\n187: private:\\n188:   std::shared_ptr<ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl> listener_factory_context_base_;\\n189:   ListenerImpl& listener_impl_;\\n190: };\\n191: \\n192: /**\\n193:  * Maps proto config to runtime config for a listener with a network filter chain.\\n194:  */\\n195: class ListenerImpl final : public Network::ListenerConfig,\\n196:                            public Network::FilterChainFactory,\\n197:                            Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\\n198: public:\\n199:   /**\\n200:    * Create a new listener.\\n201:    * @param config supplies the configuration proto.\\n202:    * @param version_info supplies the xDS version of the listener.\\n203:    * @param parent supplies the owning manager.\\n204:    * @param name supplies the listener name.\\n205:    * @param added_via_api supplies whether the listener can be updated or removed.\\n206:    * @param workers_started supplies whether the listener is being added before or after workers\\n207:    *        have been started. This controls various behavior related to init management.\\n208:    * @param hash supplies the hash to use for duplicate checking.\\n209:    * @param concurrency is the number of listeners instances to be created.\\n210:    */\\n211:   static absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>>\\n212:   create(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config, const std::string& version_info,\\n213:          ListenerManagerImpl& parent, const std::string& name, bool added_via_api,\\n214:          bool workers_started, uint64_t hash);\\n215:   ~ListenerImpl() override;\\n216: \\n217:   // TODO(lambdai): Explore using the same ListenerImpl object to execute in place filter chain\\n218:   // update.\\n219:   /**\\n220:    * Execute in place filter chain update. The filter chain update is less expensive than full\\n221:    * listener update because connections may not need to be drained.\\n222:    */\\n223:   absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>>\\n224:   newListenerWithFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n225:                              bool workers_started, uint64_t hash);\\n226:   /**\\n227:    * Determine if in place filter chain update could be executed at this moment.\\n228:    */\\n229:   bool supportUpdateFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& new_config,\\n230:                                 bool worker_started);\\n231: \\n232:   /**\\n233:    * Run the callback on each filter chain that exists in this listener but not in the passed\\n234:    * listener config.\\n235:    */\\n236:   void diffFilterChain(const ListenerImpl& another_listener,\\n237:                        std::function<void(Network::DrainableFilterChain&)> callback);\\n238: \\n239:   /**\\n240:    * Helper functions to determine whether a listener is blocked for update or remove.\\n241:    */\\n242:   bool blockUpdate(uint64_t new_hash) { return new_hash == hash_ || !added_via_api_; }\\n243:   bool blockRemove() { return !added_via_api_; }\\n244: \\n245:   const std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>& addresses() const {\\n246:     return addresses_;\\n247:   }\\n248:   const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config() const { return config_; ensureSocketOptions(options);\\n419:     Network::Socket::appendOptions(options, append_options);\\n420:   }\\n421: \\n422:   ListenerManagerImpl& parent_;\\n423:   std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr> addresses_;\\n424:   const Network::Socket::Type socket_type_;\\n425: \\n426:   std::vector<Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr> socket_factories_;\\n427:   const bool bind_to_port_;\\n428:   const bool mptcp_enabled_;\\n429:   const bool hand_off_restored_destination_connections_;\\n430:   const uint32_t per_connection_buffer_limit_bytes_;\\n431:   const uint64_t listener_tag_;\\n432:   const std::string name_;\\n433:   const bool added_via_api_;\\n434:   const bool workers_started_;\\n435:   const uint64_t hash_;\\n436:   const uint32_t tcp_backlog_size_;\\n437:   const uint32_t max_connections_to_accept_per_socket_event_;\\n438:   ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor_;\\n439:   const bool ignore_global_conn_limit_;\\n440:   const bool bypass_overload_manager_;\\n441: \\n442:   // A target is added to Server's InitManager if workers_started_ is false.\\n443:   Init::TargetImpl listener_init_target_;\\n444:   // This init manager is populated with targets from the filter chain factories, namely\\n445:   // RdsRouteConfigSubscription::init_target_, so the listener can wait for route configs.\\n446:   std::unique_ptr<Init::Manager> dynamic_init_manager_;\\n447: \\n448:   Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList listener_filter_factories_;\\n449:   std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb> udp_listener_filter_factories_;\\n450:   Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList quic_listener_filter_factories_;\\n451:   AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector access_logs_;\\n452:   const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener config_;\\n453:   const std::string version_info_;\\n454:   // Using std::vector instead of hash map for supporting multiple zero port addresses.\\n455:   std::vector<Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr> listen_socket_options_list_;\\n456:   const std::chrono::milliseconds listener_filters_timeout_;\\n457:   const bool continue_on_listener_filters_timeout_;\\n458:   std::shared_ptr<UdpListenerConfigImpl> udp_listener_config_;\\n459:   std::unique_ptr<Network::InternalListenerConfig> internal_listener_config_;\\n460:   // The key is the address string, the value is the address specific connection balancer.\\n461:   // TODO (soulxu): Add hash support for address, then needn't a string address as key anymore.\\n462:   absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, Network::ConnectionBalancerSharedPtr> connection_balancers_;\\n463:   std::shared_ptr<PerListenerFactoryContextImpl> listener_factory_context_;\\n464:   std::unique_ptr<FilterChainManagerImpl> filter_chain_manager_;\\n465:   const bool reuse_port_;\\n466: \\n467:   // Per-listener connection limits are only specified via runtime.\\n468:   //\\n469:   // TODO (tonya11en): Move this functionality into the overload manager.\\n470:   const std::string cx_limit_runtime_key_;\\n471:   std::shared_ptr<BasicResourceLimitImpl> open_connections_;\\n472: \\n473:   // This init watcher, if workers_started_ is false, notifies the \\\"parent\\\" listener manager when\\n474:   // listener initialization is complete.\\n475:   // Important: local_init_watcher_ must be the last field in the class to avoid unexpected\\n476:   // watcher callback during the destroy of ListenerImpl.\\n477:   Init::WatcherImpl local_init_watcher_;\\n478:   std::shared_ptr<Server::Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl>\\n479:       transport_factory_context_;\\n480: \\n481:   Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names_;\\n482:   MissingListenerConfigStats missing_listener_config_stats_;\\n483: \\n484:   // to access ListenerManagerImpl::factory_.\\n485:   friend class ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder;\\n486: };\\n487: \\n488: } // namespace Server\\n489: } // namespace Envoy\\n490:     "content": "1: #pragma once\n2: \n3: #include <memory>\n4: \n5: #include \"envoy/access_log/access_log.h\"\n6: #include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n7: #include
