  "content": "#pragma once\n\n#include <memory>\n\n#include \"envoy/admin/v3/config_dump.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/address.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/listener/v3/listener.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/listener/v3/listener_components.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n#include \"envoy/network/listen_socket.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/api_listener.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/filter_config.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/instance.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/listener_manager.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/transport_socket_config.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/worker.h\"\n#include \"envoy/stats/scope.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/config/well_known_names.h\"\n#include \"source/common/filter/config_discovery_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_factory_context_callback.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/lds_api.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/quic/quic_stat_names.h\"\n#include \"source/server/listener_manager_factory.h\"\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Server {\n\nnamespace Configuration {\nclass TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl;\n}\n\nclass ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder;\n\n/**\n * Prod implementation of ListenerComponentFactory that creates real sockets and attempts to fetch\n * sockets from the parent process via the hot restarter. The filter factory list is created from\n * statically registered filters.\n */\nclass ProdListenerComponentFactory : public ListenerComponentFactory,\n                                     Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\npublic:\n  ProdListenerComponentFactory(Instance& server) : server_(server) {}\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createNetworkFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList> createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\n      Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context,\n      Filter::NetworkFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context,\n      Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createUdpListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb>>\n  createUdpListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context);\n\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createQuicListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList>\n  createQuicListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context,\n      Filter::QuicListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n\n  static Network::ListenerFilterMatcherSharedPtr\n  createListenerFilterMatcher(const envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter& listener_filter);\n\n  // Server::ListenerComponentFactory\n  LdsApiPtr createLdsApi(const envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource& lds_config,\n                         const xds::core::v3::ResourceLocator* lds_resources_locator) override {\n    return std::make_unique<LdsApiImpl>(\n        lds_config, lds_resources_locator, server_.clusterManager(), server_.initManager(),\n        *server_.stats().rootScope(), server_.listenerManager(),\n        server_.messageValidationContext().dynamicValidationVisitor());\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList> createNetworkFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\n      Server::Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context) override {\n    return createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, filter_chain_factory_context,\n                                              network_config_provider_manager_);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n    return createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context,\n                                               tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb>>\n  createUdpListenerFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n    return createUdpListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList> createQuicListenerFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n    return createQuicListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context,\n                                                   quic_listener_config_provider_manager_);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> createListenSocket(\n      Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address, Network::Socket::Type socket_type,\n      const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options, BindType bind_type,\n      const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t worker_index) override;\n\n  DrainManagerPtr\n  createDrainManager(envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::DrainType drain_type) override;\n  uint64_t nextListenerTag() override { return next_listener_tag_++; }\n  Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl*\n  getTcpListenerConfigProviderManager() override {\n    return &tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_;\n  }\n\nprivate:\n  Instance& server_;\n  uint64_t next_listener_tag_{1};\n  Filter::NetworkFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl network_config_provider_manager_;\n  Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_;\n  Filter::QuicListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl quic_listener_config_provider_manager_;\n};\n\nclass ListenerImpl;\nusing ListenerImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>;\n\n/**\n * All listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\n */\n#define ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(COUNTER, GAUGE)                                                 \\\n  COUNTER(listener_added)                                                                          \\\n  COUNTER(listener_create_failure)                                                                 \\\n  COUNTER(listener_create_success)                                                                 \\\n  COUNTER(listener_in_place_updated)                                                               \\\n  COUNTER(listener_modified)                                                                       \\\n  COUNTER(listener_removed)                                                                        \\\n  COUNTER(listener_stopped)                                                                        \\\n  GAUGE(total_filter_chains_draining, NeverImport)                                                 \\\n  GAUGE(total_listeners_active, NeverImport)                                                       \\\n  GAUGE(total_listeners_draining, NeverImport)                                                     \\\n  GAUGE(total_listeners_warming, NeverImport)                                                      \\\n  GAUGE(workers_started, NeverImport)\n\n/**\n * Struct definition for all listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\n */\nstruct ListenerManagerStats {\n  ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT, GENERATE_GAUGE_STRUCT)\n};\n\n/**\n * Provides the draining filter chains and the functionality to schedule listener destroy.\n */\nclass DrainingFilterChainsManager {\npublic:\n  DrainingFilterChainsManager(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n                              uint64_t workers_pending_removal);\n  uint64_t getDrainingListenerTag() const { return draining_listener_->listenerTag(); }\n  const std::list<const Network::FilterChain*>& getDrainingFilterChains() const {\n    return draining_filter_chains_;\n  }\n  ListenerImpl& getDrainingListener() const { return *draining_listener_; }\n  uint64_t decWorkersPendingRemoval() { return --workers_pending_removal_; }\n\n  // Schedule listener destroy.\n  void startDrainSequence(std::chrono::seconds drain_time, Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\n                          std::function<void()> completion) {\n    drain_sequence_completion_ = completion;\n    ASSERT(!drain_timer_);\n\n    drain_timer_ = dispatcher.createTimer([this]() -> void { drain_sequence_completion_(); });\n    drain_timer_->enableTimer(drain_time);\n  }\n\n  void addFilterChainToDrain(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain) {\n    draining_filter_chains_.push_back(&filter_chain);\n  }\n\n  uint32_t numDrainingFilterChains() const { return draining_filter_chains_.size(); }\n\nprivate:\n  ListenerImplPtr draining_listener_;\n  std::list<const Network::FilterChain*> draining_filter_chains_;\n\n  uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\n  Event::TimerPtr drain_timer_;\n  std::function<void()> drain_sequence_completion_;\n};\n\n/**\n * Implementation of ListenerManager.\n */\nclass ListenerOrchestrator : public ListenerManager, Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\npublic:\n  ListenerOrchestrator(Instance& server, std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\n                       WorkerFactory& worker_factory, bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\n                       Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names);\n\n  void onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener);\n  void inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener);\n\n  // Server::ListenerManager\n  absl::StatusOr<bool> addOrUpdateListener(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n                                           const std::string& version_info,\n                                           bool added_via_api) override;\n  void createLdsApi(const envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource& lds_config,\n                    const xds::core::v3::ResourceLocator* lds_resources_locator) override {\n    ASSERT(lds_api_ == nullptr);\n    lds_api_ = factory_->createLdsApi(lds_config, lds_resources_locator);\n  }\n  std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n  listeners(ListenerState state = ListenerState::ACTIVE) override;\n  uint64_t numConnections() const override;\n  bool removeListener(const std::string& listener_name) override;\n  absl::Status startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog, std::function<void()> callback) override;\n  void stopListeners(StopListenersType stop_listeners_type,\n                     const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) override;\n  void stopWorkers() override;\n  void beginListenerUpdate() override { error_state_tracker_.clear(); }\n  void endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_state) override;\n  bool isWorkerStarted() override { return workers_started_; }\n  Http::Context& httpContext() { return server_.httpContext(); }\n  ApiListenerOptRef apiListener() override;\n\n  Quic::QuicStatNames& quicStatNames() { return quic_stat_names_; }\n\n  Instance& server_;\n  std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory> factory_;\n\nprivate:\n  using ListenerList = std::list<ListenerImplPtr>;\n  /**\n   * Callback invoked when a listener initialization is completed on worker.\n   */\n  using ListenerCompletionCallback = std::function<void()>;\n\n  absl::StatusOr<bool>\n  addOrUpdateListenerInternal(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n                              const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api,\n                              const std::string& name);\n  bool removeListenerInternal(const std::string& listener_name, bool dynamic_listeners_only);\n\n  struct DrainingListener {\n    DrainingListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener, uint64_t workers_pending_removal)\n        : listener_(std::move(listener)), workers_pending_removal_(workers_pending_removal) {}\n\n    ListenerImplPtr listener_;\n    uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\n  };\n\n  bool doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener);\n  void addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker, absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\n                           ListenerImpl& listener, ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback);\n\n  ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr dumpListenerConfigs(const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher);\n  static ListenerManagerStats generateStats(Stats::Scope& scope);\n  static bool hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\n                                               const ListenerImpl& listener);\n  void updateWarmingActiveGauges() {\n    // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\n    // restart.\n    stats_.total_listeners_warming_.set(warming_listeners_.size());\n    stats_.total_listeners_active_.set(active_listeners_.size());\n  }\n  bool listenersStopped(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n    // Currently all listeners in a given direction are stopped because of the way admin\n    // drain_listener functionality is implemented. This needs to be revisited, if that changes - if\n    // we support drain by listener name,for example.\n    return stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::All ||\n           (stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::InboundOnly &&\n            config.traffic_direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND);\n  }\n  void incListenerCreateFailureStat() {\n    // TODO(mattklein123): Now that we no longer create sockets and apply options on workers,\n    // this stat is confusingly named and should be more specific, however it's unclear if it's\n    // worth changing.\n    stats_.listener_create_failure_.inc();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Mark a listener for draining. The listener will no longer be considered active but will remain\n   * present to allow connection draining.\n   * @param listener supplies the listener to drain.\n   */\n  void drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener);\n\n  /**\n   * Start to draining filter chains that are owned by draining listener but not owned by\n   * new_listener. The new listener should have taken over the listener socket and partial of the\n   * filter chains from listener. This method is used by in place filter chain update.\n   * @param draining_listener supplies the listener to be replaced.\n   * @param new_listener supplies the new listener config which is going to replace the draining\n   * listener.\n   */\n  void drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener, ListenerImpl& new_listener);\n\n  /**\n   * Stop a listener. The listener will stop accepting new connections and its socket will be\n   * closed.\n   * @param listener supplies the listener to stop.\n   * @param options additional options to be passed through to shutdownListener.\n   * @param completion supplies the completion to be called when all workers are stopped accepting\n   * new connections. This completion is called on the main thread.\n   */\n  void stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n                    const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n                    std::function<void()> completion);\n\n  /**\n   * Get a listener by name. This routine is used because listeners have inherent order in static\n   * configuration and especially for tests. Thus, we can't use a map.\n   * @param listeners supplies the listener list to look in.\n   * @param name supplies the name to search for.\n   */\n  ListenerList::iterator getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name);\n\n  absl::Status setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name, ListenerImpl& listener);\n  absl::Status createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener);\n\n  void maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener);\n  absl::Status setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\n                                             const ListenerImpl& existing_listener);\n\n  ApiListenerPtr api_listener_;\n  // Active listeners are listeners that are currently accepting new connections on the workers.\n  ListenerList active_listeners_;\n  // Warming listeners are listeners that may need further initialization via the listener's init\n  // manager. For example, RDS, or in the future KDS. Once a listener is done warming it will\n  // be transitioned to active.\n  ListenerList warming_listeners_;\n  // Draining listeners are listeners that are in the process of being drained and removed. They\n  // go through two phases where first the workers stop accepting new connections and existing\n  // connections are drained. Then after that time period the listener is removed from all workers\n  // and any remaining connections are closed.\n  std::list<DrainingListener> draining_listeners_;\n  std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager> draining_filter_chains_manager_;\n\n  std::vector<WorkerPtr> workers_;\n  bool workers_started_{};\n  absl::optional<StopListenersType> stop_listeners_type_;\n  Stats::ScopeSharedPtr scope_;\n  ListenerManagerStats stats_;\n  ConfigTracker::EntryOwnerPtr config_tracker_entry_;\n  LdsApiPtr lds_api_;\n  const bool enable_dispatcher_stats_{};\n  using UpdateFailureState = envoy::admin::v3::UpdateFailureState;\n  absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, std::unique_ptr<UpdateFailureState>> error_state_tracker_;\n  FailureStates overall_error_state_;\n  Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names_;\n};\n\nclass ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder : public FilterChainFactoryBuilder {\npublic:\n  ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder(\n      ListenerImpl& listener, Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context);\n\n  absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\n  buildFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n                   FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) const override;\n\nprivate:\n  absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr> buildFilterChainInternal(\n      const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n      Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContextPtr&& filter_chain_factory_context) const;\n\n  ListenerImpl& listener_;\n  ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validator_;\n  ListenerComponentFactory& listener_component_factory_;\n  Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context_;\n};\n\nclass DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl : public ListenerManagerFactory {\npublic:\n  std::unique_ptr<ListenerManager>\n  createListenerManager(Instance& server, std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\n                        WorkerFactory& worker_factory, bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\n                        Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names) override {\n    return std::make_unique<ListenerOrchestrator>(server, std::move(factory), worker_factory,\n                                                   enable_dispatcher_stats, quic_stat_names);\n  }\n  std::string name() const override {\n    return Config::ServerExtensionValues::get().DEFAULT_LISTENER;\n  }\n  ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr createEmptyConfigProto() override {\n    return std::make_unique<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerManager>();\n  }\n};\n\nDECLARE_FACTORY(DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl);\n\n} // namespace Server\n} // namespace Envoy\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.h"
}

{
  "content": "#pragma once\n\n#include <memory>\n\n#include \"envoy/admin/v3/config_dump.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/address.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/listener/v3/listener.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/listener/v3/listener_components.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n#include \"envoy/network/listen_socket.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/api_listener.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/filter_config.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/instance.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/listener_manager.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/transport_socket_config.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/worker.h\"\n#include \"envoy/stats/scope.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/config/well_known_names.h\"\n#include \"source/common/filter/config_discovery_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_factory_context_callback.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/lds_api.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/quic/quic_stat_names.h\"\n#include \"source/server/listener_manager_factory.h\"\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Server {\n\nnamespace Configuration {\nclass TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl;\n}\n\nclass ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder;\n\n/**\n * Prod implementation of ListenerComponentFactory that creates real sockets and attempts to fetch\n * sockets from the parent process via the hot restarter. The filter factory list is created from\n * statically registered filters.\n */\nclass ProdListenerComponentFactory : public ListenerComponentFactory,\n                                     Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\npublic:\n  ProdListenerComponentFactory(Instance& server) : server_(server) {}\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createNetworkFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList> createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\n      Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context,\n      Filter::NetworkFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const config_provider_manager);\n\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createUdpListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context);\n\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createQuicListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList>\n  createQuicListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n createListenerFilterMatcher(const envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter& listener_filter);\n\n  // Server::ListenerComponentFactory\n  LdsApiPtr createLdsApi(const envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource& lds_config,\n                         const xds::core::v3::ResourceLocator* lds_resources_locator) override {\n    return std::make_unique<LdsApiImpl>(\n        lds_config, lds_resources_locator, server_.clusterManager(), server_.initManager(),\n        *server_.stats().rootScope(), server_.listenerManager(),\n        server_.messageValidationContext().dynamicValidationVisitor());\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList> createNetworkFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\n      Server::Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context) override {\n    return createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, filter_chain_factory_context,\n                                              network_config_provider_manager_);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n    return createUdpListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList> createQuicListenerFilterFactoryList(\n      const createQuicListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context,\n                                                   quic_listener_config_provider_manager_);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> override;\n  uint64_t nextListenerTag() override { return next_listener_tag_++; }\n  Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl*\n  getTcpListenerConfigProviderManager() override {\n    return ListenerImpl;\nusing ListenerImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>;\n\n/**\n * All listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\n */\n#define ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(COUNTER, GAUGE)                                                 \\\n  COUNTER(listener_added)                                                                          \\\n  COUNTER(listener_create_failure)                                                                 \\\n  COUNTER(listener_create_success)                                                                 \\\n  COUNTER(listener_in_place_updated)                                                               \\\n  COUNTER(listener_modified)                                                                       \\\n  COUNTER(listener_removed)                                                                        \\\n  COUNTER(listener_stopped)                                                                        \\\n  GAUGE(total_filter_chains_draining, NeverImport)                                                 \\\n  GAUGE(total_listeners_active, NeverImport)                                                       \\\n  GAUGE(total_listeners_draining, NeverImport)                                                     \\\n  GAUGE(total_listeners_warming, NeverImport)                                                      \\\n  GAUGE(workers_started, NeverImport)\n\n/**\n * Struct definition for all listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\n */\nstruct ListenerManagerStats {\n  ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT, GENERATE_GAUGE_STRUCT)\n};\n\n/**\n * Provides the draining filter chains and the functionality to schedule listener destroy.\n */\nclass DrainingFilterChainsManager {\npublic:\n  DrainingFilterChainsManager(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n                              uint64_t workers_pending_removal);\n  uint64_t getDrainingListenerTag() const { return draining_listener_->listenerTag(); }\n  const std::list<const Network::FilterChain*>& getDrainingFilterChains() const {\n    return draining_filter_chains_;\n  }\n  ListenerImpl& getDrainingListener() const { return *draining_listener_; }\n  uint64_t decWorkersPendingRemoval() { return --workers_pending_removal_; }\n\n  // Schedule listener destroy.\n  void startDrainSequence(std::chrono::seconds drain_time, Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\n                          std::function<void()> completion) {\n    drain_sequence_completion_ = completion;\n    ASSERT(!drain_timer_);\n\n    drain_timer_ = dispatcher.createTimer([this]() -> void { drain_sequence_completion_(); });\n    drain_timer_->enableTimer(drain_time);\n  }\n\n  void addFilterChainToDrain(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain) {\n    draining_filter_chains_.push_back(&filter_chain);\n  }\n\n  uint32_t numDrainingFilterChains() const { return draining_filter_chains_.size(); }\n\nprivate:\n  ListenerImplPtr draining_listener_;\n  std::list<const Network::FilterChain*> draining_filter_chains_;\n\n  uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\n  Event::TimerPtr drain_timer_;\n  std::function<void()> drain_sequence_completion_;\n};\n\n/**\n * Implementation of ListenerManager.\n */\nclass ListenerOrchestrator : public ListenerManager, Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\npublic:\n  ListenerOrchestrator(Instance& server, std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\n                       WorkerFactory& worker_factory, bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\n                       Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names);\n\n  void onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener);\n  void inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener);\n\n  // Server::ListenerManager\n  absl::StatusOr<bool> addOrUpdateListener(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n                                           const std::string& version_info,\n                                           bool added_via_api) override;\n  void createLdsApi(const envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource& lds_config,\n                    const xds::core::v3::ResourceLocator* lds_resources_locator) override {\n    ASSERT(lds_api_ == nullptr);\n    lds_api_ = factory_->createLdsApi(lds_config, lds_resources_locator);\n  }\n  std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n  listeners(ListenerState state = ListenerState::ACTIVE) override;\n  uint64_t numConnections() const override;\n  bool removeListener(const std::string& listener_name) override;\n  absl::Status startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog, std::function<void()> callback) override;\n  void stopListeners(StopListenersType stop_listeners_type,\n                     const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) override;\n  void stopWorkers() override;\n  void beginListenerUpdate() override { error_state_tracker_.clear(); }\n  void endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_state) override;\n  bool isWorkerStarted() override { return workers_started_; }\n  Http::Context& httpContext() { return server_.httpContext(); }\n  ApiListenerOptRef apiListener() override;\n\n  Quic::QuicStatNames& quicStatNames() { return quic_stat_names_; }\n\n  Instance& server_;\n  std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory> factory_;\n\nprivate:\n  using ListenerList = std::list<ListenerImplPtr>;\n  /**\n   * Callback invoked when a listener initialization is completed on worker.\n   */\n  using ListenerCompletionCallback = std::function<void()>;\n\n  absl::StatusOr<bool>\n  addOrUpdateListenerInternal(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n                              const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api,\n                              const std::string& name);\n  bool removeListenerInternal(const std::string& listener_name, bool dynamic_listeners_only);\n\n  struct DrainingListener {\n    DrainingListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener, uint64_t workers_pending_removal)\n        : listener_(std::move(listener)), workers_pending_removal_(workers_pending_removal) {}\n\n    ListenerImplPtr listener_;\n    uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\n  };\n\n  bool doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener);\n  void addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker, absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\n                           ListenerImpl& listener, ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback);\n\n  ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr dumpListenerConfigs(const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher);\n  static ListenerManagerStats generateStats(Stats::Scope& scope);\n  static bool hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\n                                               const ListenerImpl& listener);\n  void updateWarmingActiveGauges() {\n    // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\n    // restart.\n    stats_.total_listeners_warming_.set(warming_listeners_.size());\n    stats_.total_listeners_active_.set(active_listeners_.size());\n  }\n  bool listenersStopped(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n    // Currently all listeners in a given direction are stopped because of the way admin\n    // drain_listener functionality is implemented. This needs to be revisited, if that changes - if\n    // we support drain by listener name,for example.\n    return stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::All ||\n           (stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::InboundOnly &&\n            config.traffic_direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND);\n  }\n  void incListenerCreateFailureStat() {\n    // TODO(mattklein123): Now that we no longer create sockets and apply options on workers,\n    // this stat is confusingly named and should be more specific, however it's unclear if it's\n    // worth changing.\n    stats_.listener_create_failure_.inc();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Mark a listener for draining. The listener will no longer be considered active but will remain\n   * present to allow connection draining.\n   * @param listener supplies the listener to drain.\n   */\n  void drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener);\n\n  /**\n   * Start to draining filter chains that are owned by draining listener but not owned by\n   * new_listener. The new listener should have taken over the listener socket and partial of the\n   * filter chains from listener. This method is used by in place filter chain update.\n   * @param draining_listener supplies the listener to be replaced.\n   * @param new_listener supplies the new listener config which is going to replace the draining\n   * listener.\n   */\n  void drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener, ListenerImpl& new_listener);\n\n  /**\n   * Stop a listener. The listener will stop accepting new connections and its socket will be\n   * closed.\n   * @param listener supplies the listener to stop.\n   * @param options additional options to be passed through to shutdownListener.\n   * @param completion supplies the completion to be called when all workers are stopped accepting\n   * new connections. This completion is called on the main thread.\n   */\n  void stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n                    const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n                    std::function<void()> completion);\n\n  /**\n   * Get a listener by name. This routine is used because listeners have inherent order in static\n   * configuration and especially for tests. Thus, we can't use a map.\n   * @param listeners supplies the listener list to look in.\n   * @param name supplies the name to search for.\n   */\n  ListenerList::iterator getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name);\n\n  absl::Status setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name, ListenerImpl& listener);\n  absl::Status createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener);\n\n  void maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener);\n  absl::Status setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\n                                             const ListenerImpl& existing_listener);\n\n  ApiListenerPtr api_listener_;\n  // Active listeners are listeners that are currently accepting new connections on the workers.\n  ListenerList active_listeners_;\n  // Warming listeners are listeners that may need further initialization via the listener's init\n  // manager. For example, RDS, or in the future KDS. Once a listener is done warming it will\n  // be transitioned to active.\n  ListenerList warming_listeners_;\n  // Draining listeners are listeners that are in the process of being drained and removed. They\n  // go through two phases where first the workers stop accepting new connections and existing\n  // connections are drained. Then after that time period the listener is removed from all workers\n  // and any remaining connections are closed.\n  std::list<DrainingListener> draining_listeners_;\n  std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager> draining_filter_chains_manager_;\n\n  std::vector<WorkerPtr> workers_;\n  bool workers_started_{};\n  absl::optional<StopListenersType> stop_listeners_type_;\n  Stats::ScopeSharedPtr scope_;\n  ListenerManagerStats stats_;\n  ConfigTracker::EntryOwnerPtr config_tracker_entry_;\n  LdsApiPtr lds_api_;\n  const bool enable_dispatcher_stats_{};\n  using UpdateFailureState = envoy::admin::v3::UpdateFailureState;\n  absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, std::unique_ptr<UpdateFailureState>> error_state_tracker_;\n  FailureStates overall_error_state_;\n  Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names_;\n};\n\nclass ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder : public FilterChainFactoryBuilder {\npublic:\n  ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder(\n      ListenerImpl& listener, Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context);\n\n  absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\n  buildFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n                   FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) const override;\n\nprivate:\n  absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr> buildFilterChainInternal(\n      const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n      Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContextPtr&& filter_chain_factory_context) const;\n\n  ListenerImpl& listener_;\n  ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validator_;\n  ListenerComponentFactory& listener_component_factory_;\n  Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context_;\n};\n\nclass DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl : public ListenerManagerFactory {\npublic:\n  std::unique_ptr<ListenerManager>\n  createListenerManager(Instance& server, std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\n                        WorkerFactory& worker_factory, bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\n                        Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names) override {\n    return std::make_unique<ListenerOrchestrator>(server, std::move(factory), worker_factory,\n                                                   enable_dispatcher_stats, quic_stat_names);\n  }\n  std::string name() const override {\n    return Config::ServerExtensionValues::get().DEFAULT_LISTENER;\n  }\n  ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr createEmptyConfigProto() override {\n    return std::make_unique<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerManager>();\n  }\n};\n\nDECLARE_FACTORY(DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl);\n\n} // namespace Server\n} // namespace Envoy\n",

draining_group]() -> void {\\n862:         draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\\n863:             absl::StrCat(\\\"removing draining filter chains from listener \\\",\\n864: draining_group->getDrainingFilterChains(),\\n870:               [this, draining_group]() -> void {\\n871:                 // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. draining_filter_chains_manager_.erase(draining_group);\\n882:                   }\\n883:                 });\\n884:               });\\n885:         }\\n886:       });\\n887: name);\\n908:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\\n909:   if ((existing_warming_listener == warming_listeners_.end() ||\\n910: directly.\\n925:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\\n926:     // Listeners in active_listeners_ are added to workers after workers start, so we drain\\n927:     // listeners only after this occurs.\\n928:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\\n929:     // state.\\n930:     auto listener = ListenerManagerImpl::startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog,\\n943:                                                std::function<void()> callback) {\\n944:   ENVOY_LOG(info, \\\"all dependencies initialized. starting workers\\\");\\n945:   ASSERT(!workers_started_);\\n946:   workers_started_ = true;\\n947:   uint32_t i = 0;\\n948: \\n949:   absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\\n950:   std::function<void()> worker_started_running = [&workers_waiting_to_run]() {\\n951:     workers_waiting_to_run.DecrementCount();\\n952:   };\\n953: \\n954:   // We can not use \\\"Cleanup\\\" to simplify this logic here, because it results in a issue if Envoy is\\n955:   // killed before workers are actually started. std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use iterators directly.\\n961:   for (auto listener_it = active_listeners_.begin(); listener_it != active_listeners_.end();) {\\n962:     auto& listener = *listener_it;\\n963:     listener_it++;\\n964: incListenerCreateFailureStat();\\n970:       removeListenerInternal(listener->name(), false);\\n971:       continue;\\n972:     }\\n973:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\\n974: == 0) {\\n977:                               stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\\n978:                               callback();\\n979:                             }\\n980: worker_started_running);\\n986:     if (enable_dispatcher_stats_) {\\n987:       worker->initializeStats(*scope_);\\n988:     }\\n989:     i++;\\n990:   }\\n991: \\n992:   // Wait for workers to start running.\\n993:   workers_waiting_to_run.Wait();\\n994: \\n995:   if (active_listeners_.empty()) {\\n996:     stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\\n997:     callback();\\n998:   }\\n999:   return absl::OkStatus();\\n1000: }\\n1001: \\n1002: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\\n1003:                                        const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\\n1004:                                        std::function<void()> callback) {\\n1005:   const auto workers_pending_stop = std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size());\\n1006:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\\n1007:     worker->stopListener(listener, options, [this, callback, workers_pending_stop]() {\\n1008:       if (--(*workers_pending_stop) == 0) {\\n1009:         server_.dispatcher().post(callback);\\n1010:       }\\n1011:     });\\n1012:   }\\n1013: }\\n1014: \\n1015: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopListeners(StopListenersType stop_listeners_type,\\n1016:                                         const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) {\\n1017: uint64_t listener_tag = listener.listenerTag();\\n1031:       stopListener(listener, options, [this, listener_tag]() {\\n1032:         stats_.listener_stopped_.inc();\\n1033:         for (auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\\n1034:           if (listener->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\\n1035:             maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener);\\n1036:           }\\n1037: ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::buildFilterChain(\\n1064:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\\n1065:     FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) const absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\\n1071: ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::buildFilterChainInternal(\\n1072:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\\n1073:     Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContextPtr&& filter_chain_factory_context) const {\\n1074:   // If the cluster doesn't have transport socket configured, then use the default \\\"raw_buffer\\\"\\n1075:   // transport socket or BoringSSL-based \\\"tls\\\" transport socket if TLS settings are configured.\\n1076:   // We copy by value first then override if necessary.\\n1077:   auto transport_socket = filter_chain.transport_socket();\\n1078:   if (!filter_chain.has_transport_socket()) {\\n1079:     envoy::extensions::transport_sockets::raw_buffer::v3::RawBuffer raw_buffer;\\n1080:     transport_socket.mutable_typed_config()->PackFrom(raw_buffer);\\n1081:     transport_socket.set_name(\\\"envoy.transport_sockets.raw_buffer\\\");\\n1082:   }\\n1083: \\n1084:   auto& config_factory = Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<\\n1085:       Server::Configuration::DownstreamTransportSocketConfigFactory>(transport_socket);\\n1086:   // The only connection oriented UDP transport protocol right now is QUIC.\\n1087:   const bool is_quic =\\n1088:       listener_.udpListenerConfig().has_value() &&\\n1089:       !listener_.udpListenerConfig()->listenerFactory().isTransportConnectionless();\\n1090: #if defined(ENVOY_ENABLE_QUIC)\\n1091:   if (is_quic &&\\n1092:       dynamic_cast<Quic::QuicServerTransportSocketConfigFactory*>(&config_factory) == nullptr) {\\n1093:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n1094:         fmt::format(\\\"error building filter chain for quic listener: wrong \\\"\\n1095:                     \\\"transport socket config specified for quic transport socket: \\\"\\n1096:                     \\\"{}. \\\\nUse QuicDownstreamTransport instead.\\\",\\n1097:                     transport_socket.DebugString()));\\n1098:   }\\n1099:   const std::string hcm_str =\\n1100:       \\\"type.googleapis.com/\\\"\\n1101:       \\\"envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager\\\";\\n1102:   if (is_quic &&\\n1103:       (filter_chain.filters().empty() ||\\n1104:        filter_chain.filters(filter_chain.filters().size() - 1).typed_config().type_url() !=\\n1105:            hcm_str)) {\\n1106:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n1107:         fmt::format(\\\"error building network filter chain for quic listener: requires \\\"\\n1108:                     \\\"http_connection_manager filter to be last in the chain.\\\"));\\n1109:   }\\n1110: #else\\n1111:   // When QUIC is compiled out it should not be possible to configure either the QUIC transport\\n1112:   // socket or the QUIC listener and get to this point.\\n1113:   ASSERT(!is_quic);\\n1114: #endif\\n1115:   ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr message =\\n1116:       Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(transport_socket, validator_, config_factory);\\n1117: \\n1118:   std::vector<std::string> server_names(filter_chain.filter_chain_match().server_names().begin(),\\n1119:                                         filter_chain.filter_chain_match().server_names().end());\\n1120: \\n1121:   auto factory_or_error = config_factory.createTransportSocketFactory(*message, factory_context_,\\n1122:                                                                       std::move(server_names));\\n1123:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(factory_or_error.status());\\n1124:   auto factory_list_or_error = listener_component_factory_.createNetworkFilterFactoryList(\\n1125:       filter_chain.filters(), *filter_chain_factory_context);\\n1126:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(factory_list_or_error.status());\\n1127: \\n1128:   auto filter_chain_res = std::make_shared<FilterChainImpl>(\\n1129:       std::move(factory_or_error.value()), std::move(*factory_list_or_error),\\n1130:       std::chrono::milliseconds(\\n1131:           PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(filter_chain, transport_socket_connect_timeout, 0)),\\n1132:       filter_chain.name());\\n1133: \\n1134:   filter_chain_res->setFilterChainFactoryContext(std::move(filter_chain_factory_context));\\n1135:   return filter_chain_res;\\n1136: }\\n1137: \\n1138: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name,\\n1139:                                                                 ListenerImpl& listener) {\\n1140:   if (hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(warming_listeners_, listener) ||\\n1141:       hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(active_listeners_, listener)) {\\n1142:     const std::string message =\\n1143:         fmt::format(\\\"error adding listener: '{}' has duplicate address '{}' as existing listener\\\",\\n1144:                     name, absl::StrJoin(listener.addresses(), \\\",\\\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()));\\n1145:     ENVOY_LOG(warn, \\\"{}\\\", message);\\n1146:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(message);\\n1147:   }\\n1148: \\n1149:   // Search through draining listeners to see if there is a listener that has a socket factory for\\n1150:   // the same address we are configured for. This is an edge case, but\\n1151:   // may happen if a listener is removed and then added back with a same or different name and\\n1152:   // intended to listen on the same address. This should work and not fail.\\n1153:   const ListenerImpl* draining_listener_ptr = nullptr;\\n1154:   auto existing_draining_listener =\\n1155:       std::find_if(draining_listeners_.cbegin(), draining_listeners_.cend(),\\n1156:                    [&listener](const DrainingListener& draining_listener) {\\n1157:                      return draining_listener.listener_->listenSocketFactories()[0]\\n1158:                                 ->getListenSocket(0)\\n1159:                                 ->isOpen() &&\\n1160:                             listener.hasCompatibleAddress(*draining_listener.listener_);\\n1161:                    });\\n1162: \\n1163:   if (existing_draining_listener != draining_listeners_.cend()) {\\n1164:     existing_draining_listener->listener_->debugLog(\\\"clones listener sockets\\\");\\n1165:     draining_listener_ptr = existing_draining_listener->listener_.get();\\n1166:   } else {\\n1167:     auto existing_draining_filter_chain = std::find_if(\\n1168:         draining_filter_chains_manager_.cbegin(), draining_filter_chains_manager_.cend(),\\n1169:         [&listener](const DrainingFilterChainsManager& draining_filter_chain) {\\n1170:           return draining_filter_chain.getDrainingListener()\\n1171:                      .listenSocketFactories()[0]\\n1172:                      ->getListenSocket(0)\\n1173:                      ->isOpen() &&\\n1174:                  listener.hasCompatibleAddress(draining_filter_chain.getDrainingListener());\\n1175:         });\\n1176: \\n1177:     if (existing_draining_filter_chain != draining_filter_chains_manager_.cend()) {\\n1178:       existing_draining_filter_chain->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\\\"clones listener socket\\\");\\n1179:       draining_listener_ptr = &existing_draining_filter_chain->getDrainingListener();\\n1180:     }\\n1181:   }\\n1182: \\n1183:   if (draining_listener_ptr != nullptr) {\\n1184:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(listener.cloneSocketFactoryFrom(*draining_listener_ptr));\\n1185:   } else {\\n1186:     return createListenSocketFactory(listener);\\n1187:   }\\n1188:   return absl::OkStatus();\\n1189: }\\n1190: \\n1191: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener) {\\n1192:   Network::Socket::Type socket_type = listener.socketType();\\n1193:   ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type = ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoBind;\\n1194:   if (listener.bindToPort()) {\\n1195:     bind_type = listener.reusePort() ? ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort\\n1196:                                      : ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoReusePort;\\n1197:   }\\n1198:   absl::Status socket_status = absl::OkStatus();\\n1199:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\\n1200:     Network::SocketCreationOptions creation_options;\\n1201:     creation_options.mptcp_enabled_ = listener.mptcpEnabled();\\n1202:     for (std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>::size_type i = 0;\\n1203:          i < listener.addresses().size(); i++) {\\n1204:       auto factory_or_error = ListenSocketFactoryImpl::create(\\n1205:           *factory_, listener.addresses()[i], socket_type, listener.listenSocketOptions(i),\\n1206:           listener.name(), listener.tcpBacklogSize(), bind_type, creation_options,\\n1207:           server_.options().concurrency());\\n1208:       if (!factory_or_error.status().ok()) {\\n1209:         socket_status = factory_or_error.status();\\n1210:       } else {\\n1211:         socket_status = listener.addSocketFactory(std::move(*factory_or_error));\\n1212:       }\\n1213:       if (!socket_status.ok()) {\\n1214:         break;\\n1215:       }\\n1216:     }\\n1217:   }\\n1218:   END_TRY\\n1219:   CATCH(const EnvoyException& e, {\\n1220:     socket_status = absl::InvalidArgumentError(e.what());\\n1221:     ;\\n1222:   });\\n1223:   if (!socket_status.ok()) {\\n1224:     ENVOY_LOG(error, \\\"listener '{}' failed to bind or apply socket options: {}\\\", listener.name(),\\n1225:               socket_status.message());\\n1226:     incListenerCreateFailureStat();\\n1227:   }\\n1228:   return socket_status;\\n1229: }\\n1230: \\n1231: void ListenerManagerImpl::maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener) {\\n1232:   if (!listener.udpListenerConfig().has_value() ||\\n1233:       listener.udpListenerConfig()->listenerFactory().isTransportConnectionless()) {\\n1234:     // Close the listen sockets right away to avoid leaving TCP connections in accept queue\\n1235:     // already waiting for long timeout. However, connection-oriented UDP listeners shouldn't\\n1236:     // close the socket because they need to receive packets for existing connections via the\\n1237:     // listen sockets.\\n1238:     listener.closeAllSockets();\\n1239: \\n1240:     // In case of this listener was in-place updated previously and in the filter chains draining\\n1241:     // procedure, so close the sockets for the previous draining listener.\\n1242:     for (auto& manager : draining_filter_chains_manager_) {\\n1243:       // A listener can be in-place updated multiple times, so there may\\n1244:       // have multiple draining listeners with same tag.\\n1245:       if (manager.getDrainingListenerTag() == listener.listenerTag()) {\\n1246:         manager.getDrainingListener().closeAllSockets();\\n1247:       }\\n1248:     }\\n1249:   }\\n1250: }\\n1251: \\n1252: ApiListenerOptRef ListenerManagerImpl::apiListener() {\\n1253:   return api_listener_ ? ApiListenerOptRef(std::ref(*api_listener_)) : absl::nullopt;\\n1254: }\\n1255: \\n1256: REGISTER_FACTORY(DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl, ListenerManagerFactory);\\n1257: \\n1258: } // namespace Server\\n1259: } // namespace Envoy\\n1260: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0@75cca99ee9fea5a6b699af6a2124ec7404fe5d13/-/blob/source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.cc%3FL800-1300\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "800:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n801:     // state.\n802:     auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n803:     *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n804:     drainListener(std::move(listener));\n805:   } else {\n806:     active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(*existing_warming_listener));\n807:   }\n808: \n809:   warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n810:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n811: }\n812: \n813: void ListenerManagerImpl::inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n814:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n815:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n816:   ASSERT(existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end());\n817:   ASSERT(*existing_warming_listener != nullptr);\n818: \n819:   (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"execute in place filter chain update\");\n820: \n821:   // Now that in place filter chain update was decided, the replaced listener must be in active\n822:   // list. It requires stop/remove listener procedure cancelling the in placed update if any.\n823:   ASSERT(existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end());\n824:   ASSERT(*existing_active_listener != nullptr);\n825: \n826:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n827:     // Explicitly override the existing listener with a new listener config.\n828:     addListenerToWorker(*worker, listener.listenerTag(), listener, nullptr);\n829:   }\n830: \n831:   auto previous_listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n832:   *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n833:   // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainFilterChains` as it depends on\n834:   // their state.\n835:   drainFilterChains(std::move(previous_listener), **existing_active_listener);\n836: \n837:   warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n838:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n839: }\n840: \n841: void ListenerManagerImpl::drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n842:                                             ListenerImpl& new_listener) {\n843:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\n844:   std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager>::iterator draining_group =\n845:       draining_filter_chains_manager_.emplace(draining_filter_chains_manager_.begin(),\n846:                                               std::move(draining_listener), workers_.size());\n847:   draining_group->getDrainingListener().diffFilterChain(\n848:       new_listener, [&draining_group](Network::DrainableFilterChain& filter_chain) mutable {\n849:         filter_chain.startDraining();\n850:         draining_group->addFilterChainToDrain(filter_chain);\n851:       });\n852:   auto filter_chain_size = draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains();\n853:   stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.add(filter_chain_size);\n854:   draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n855:       absl::StrCat(\"draining \", filter_chain_size, \" filter chains in listener \",\n856:                    draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n857: \n858:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n859:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n860:   draining_group->startDrainSequence(\n861:       server_.options().drainTime(), server_.dispatcher(), [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n862:         draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n863:             absl::StrCat(\"removing draining filter chains from listener \",\n864:                          draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n865:         for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n866:           // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n867:           // to remove the filter chains.\n868:           worker->removeFilterChains(\n869:               draining_group->getDrainingListenerTag(), draining_group->getDrainingFilterChains(),\n870:               [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n871:                 // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to\n872:                 // the main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the\n873:                 // listener while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n874:                 server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_group]() -> void {\n875:                   if (draining_group->decWorkersPendingRemoval() == 0) {\n876:                     draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n877:                         absl::StrCat(\"draining filter chains from listener \",\n878:                                      draining_group->getDrainingListener().name(), \" complete\"));\n879:                     stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.sub(\n880:                         draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains());\n881:                     draining_filter_chains_manager_.erase(draining_group);\n882:                   }\n883:                 });\n884:               });\n885:         }\n886:       });\n887:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n888: }\n889: \n890: uint64_t ListenerManagerImpl::numConnections() const {\n891:   uint64_t num_connections = 0;\n892:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n893:     num_connections += worker->numConnections();\n894:   }\n895: \n896:   return num_connections;\n897: }\n898: \n899: bool ListenerManagerImpl::removeListener(const std::string& name) {\n900:   return removeListenerInternal(name, true);\n901: }\n902: \n903: bool ListenerManagerImpl::removeListenerInternal(const std::string& name,\n904:                                                  bool dynamic_listeners_only) {\n905:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin remove listener: name={}\", name);\n906: \n907:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n908:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\n909:   if ((existing_warming_listener == warming_listeners_.end() ||\n910:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_warming_listener)->blockRemove())) &&\n911:       (existing_active_listener == active_listeners_.end() ||\n912:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_active_listener)->blockRemove()))) {\n913:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"unknown/locked listener '{}'. no remove\", name);\n914:     return false;\n915:   }\n916: \n917:   // Destroy a warming listener directly.\n918:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n919:     (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"removing warming listener\");\n920:     warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n921:   }\n922: \n923:   // If there is an active listener it needs to be moved to draining after workers have started, or\n924:   // destroyed directly.\n925:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n926:     // Listeners in active_listeners_ are added to workers after workers start, so we drain\n927:     // listeners only after this occurs.\n928:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n929:     // state.\n930:     auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n931:     active_listeners_.erase(existing_active_listener);\n932:     if (workers_started_) {\n933:       drainListener(std::move(listener));\n934:     }\n935:   }\n936: \n937:   stats_.listener_removed_.inc();\n938:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n939:   return true;\n940: }\n941: \n942: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog,\n943:                                                std::function<void()> callback) {\n944:   ENVOY_LOG(info, \"all dependencies initialized. starting workers\");\n945:   ASSERT(!workers_started_);\n946:   workers_started_ = true;\n947:   uint32_t i = 0;\n948: \n949:   absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\n950:   std::function<void()> worker_started_running = [&workers_waiting_to_run]() {\n951:     workers_waiting_to_run.DecrementCount();\n952:   };\n953: \n954:   // We can not use \"Cleanup\" to simplify this logic here, because it results in a issue if Envoy is\n955:   // killed before workers are actually started. Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n956:   // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n957:   const auto listeners_pending_init =\n958:       std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use iterators directly.\n961:   for (auto listener_it = active_listeners_.begin(); listener_it != active_listeners_.end();) {\n962:     auto& listener = *listener_it;\n963:     listener_it++;\n964: \n965:     absl::StatusOr<bool> init_status = doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(*listener);\n966:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(init_status.status());\n967: \n968:     if (!*init_status) {\n969:       incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n970:       removeListenerInternal(listener->name(), false);\n971:       continue;\n972:     }\n973:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n974:       addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, *listener,\n975:                           [this, listeners_pending_init, callback]() {\n976:                             if (--(*listeners_pending_init) == 0) {\n977:                               stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n978:                               callback();\n979:                             }\n980:                           });\n981:     }\n982:   }\n983:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n984:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"starting worker {}\", i);\n985:     worker->start(guard_dog, worker_started_running);\n986:     if (enable_dispatcher_stats_) {\n987:       worker->initializeStats(*scope_);\n988:     }\n989:     i++;\n990:   }\n991: \n992:   // Wait for workers to start running.\n993:   workers_waiting_to_run.Wait();\n994: \n995:   if (active_listeners_.empty()) {\n996:     stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n997:     callback();\n998:   }\n999:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1000: }\n1001: \n1002: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n1003:                                        const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n1004:                                        std::function<void()> callback) {\n1005:   const auto workers_pending_stop = std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size());\n1006:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n1007:     worker->stopListener(listener, options, [this, callback, workers_pending_stop]() {\n1008:       if (--(*workers_pending_stop) == 0) {\n1009:         server_.dispatcher().post(callback);\n1010:       }\n1011:     });\n1012:   }\n1013: }\n1014: \n1015: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopListeners(StopListenersType stop_listeners_type,\n1016:                                         const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) {\n1017:   stop_listeners_type_ = stop_listeners_type;\n1018:   for (Network::ListenerConfig& listener : listeners()) {\n1019:     if (stop_listeners_type != StopListenersType::InboundOnly ||\n1020:         listener.listenerInfo()->direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND) {\n1021:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin stop listener: name={}\", listener.name());\n1022:       auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n1023:       // Destroy a warming listener directly.\n1024:       if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n1025:         (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"removing warming listener\");\n1026:         warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n1027:       }\n1028:       // Close the socket once all workers stopped accepting its connections.\n1029:       // This allows clients to fast fail instead of waiting in the accept queue.\n1030:       const uint64_t listener_tag = listener.listenerTag();\n1031:       stopListener(listener, options, [this, listener_tag]() {\n1032:         stats_.listener_stopped_.inc();\n1033:         for (auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n1034:           if (listener->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\n1035:             maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener);\n1036:           }\n1037:         }\n1038:       });\n1039:     }\n1040:   }\n1041: }\n1042: \n1043: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopWorkers() {\n1044:   if (!workers_started_) {\n1045:     return;\n1046:   }\n1047:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n1048:     worker->stop();\n1049:   }\n1050: }\n1051: \n1052: void ListenerManagerImpl::endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_states) {\n1053:   overall_error_state_ = std::move(failure_states);\n1054: }\n1055: \n1056: ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder(\n1057:     ListenerImpl& listener,\n1058:     Server::Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context)\n1059:     : listener_(listener), validator_(listener.validation_visitor_),\n1060:       listener_component_factory_(*listener.parent_.factory_), factory_context_(factory_context) {}\n1061: \n1062: absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\n1063: ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::buildFilterChain(\n1064:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n1065:     FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) const {\n1066:   return buildFilterChainInternal(filter_chain,\n1067:                                   context_creator.createFilterChainFactoryContext(&filter_chain));\n1068: }\n1069: \n1070: absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\n1071: ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::buildFilterChainInternal(\n1072:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n1073:     Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContextPtr&& filter_chain_factory_context) const {\n1074:   // If the cluster doesn't have transport socket configured, then use the default \"raw_buffer\"\n1075:   // transport socket or BoringSSL-based \"tls\" transport socket if TLS settings are configured.\n1076:   // We copy by value first then override if necessary.\n1077:   auto transport_socket = filter_chain.transport_socket();\n1078:   if (!filter_chain.has_transport_socket()) {\n1079:     envoy::extensions::transport_sockets::raw_buffer::v3::RawBuffer raw_buffer;\n1080:     transport_socket.mutable_typed_config()->PackFrom(raw_buffer);\n1081:     transport_socket.set_name(\"envoy.transport_sockets.raw_buffer\");\n1082:   }\n1083: \n1084:   auto& config_factory = Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<\n1085:       Server::Configuration::DownstreamTransportSocketConfigFactory>(transport_socket);\n1086:   // The only connection oriented UDP transport protocol right now is QUIC.\n1087:   const bool is_quic =\n1088:       listener_.udpListenerConfig().has_value() &&\n1089:       !listener_.udpListenerConfig()->listenerFactory().isTransportConnectionless();\n1090: #if defined(ENVOY_ENABLE_QUIC)\n1091:   if (is_quic &&\n1092:       dynamic_cast<Quic::QuicServerTransportSocketConfigFactory*>(&config_factory) == nullptr) {\n1093:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n1094:         fmt::format(\"error building filter chain for quic listener: wrong \"\n1095:                     \"transport socket config specified for quic transport socket: \"\n1096:                     \"{}. \\nUse QuicDownstreamTransport instead.\",\n1097:                     transport_socket.DebugString()));\n1098:   }\n1099:   const std::string hcm_str =\n1100:       \"type.googleapis.com/\"\n1101:       \"envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager\";\n1102:   if (is_quic &&\n1103:       (filter_chain.filters().empty() ||\n1104:        filter_chain.filters(filter_chain.filters().size() - 1).typed_config().type_url() !=\n1105:            hcm_str)) {\n1106:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n1107:         fmt::format(\"error building network filter chain for quic listener: requires \"\n1108:                     \"http_connection_manager filter to be last in the chain.\"));\n1109:   }\n1110: #else\n1111:   // When QUIC is compiled out it should not be possible to configure either the QUIC transport\n1112:   // socket or the QUIC listener and get to this point.\n1113:   ASSERT(!is_quic);\n1114: #endif\n1115:   ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr message =\n1116:       Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(transport_socket, validator_, config_factory);\n1117: \n1118:   std::vector<std::string> server_names(filter_chain.filter_chain_match().server_names().begin(),\n1119:                                         filter_chain.filter_chain_match().server_names().end());\n1120: \n1121:   auto factory_or_error = config_factory.createTransportSocketFactory(*message, factory_context_,\n1122:                                                                       std::move(server_names));\n1123:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(factory_or_error.status());\n1124:   auto factory_list_or_error = listener_component_factory_.createNetworkFilterFactoryList(\n1125:       filter_chain.filters(), *filter_chain_factory_context);\n1126:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(factory_list_or_error.status());\n1127: \n1128:   auto filter_chain_res = std::make_shared<FilterChainImpl>(\n1129:       std::move(factory_or_error.value()), std::move(*factory_list_or_error),\n1130:       std::chrono::milliseconds(\n1131:           PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(filter_chain, transport_socket_connect_timeout, 0)),\n1132:       filter_chain.name());\n1133: \n1134:   filter_chain_res->setFilterChainFactoryContext(std::move(filter_chain_factory_context));\n1135:   return filter_chain_res;\n1136: }\n1137: \n1138: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name,\n1139:                                                                 ListenerImpl& listener) {\n1140:   if (hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(warming_listeners_, listener) ||\n1141:       hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(active_listeners_, listener)) {\n1142:     const std::string message =\n1143:         fmt::format(\"error adding listener: '{}' has duplicate address '{}' as existing listener\",\n1144:                     name, absl::StrJoin(listener.addresses(), \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()));\n1145:     ENVOY_LOG(warn, \"{}\", message);\n1146:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(message);\n1147:   }\n1148: \n1149:   // Search through draining listeners to see if there is a listener that has a socket factory for\n1150:   // the same address we are configured for. This is an edge case, but\n1151:   // may happen if a listener is removed and then added back with a same or different name and\n1152:   // intended to listen on the same address. This should work and not fail.\n1153:   const ListenerImpl* draining_listener_ptr = nullptr;\n1154:   auto existing_draining_listener =\n1155:       std::find_if(draining_listeners_.cbegin(), draining_listeners_.cend(),\n1156:                    [&listener](const DrainingListener& draining_listener) {\n1157:                      return draining_listener.listener_->listenSocketFactories()[0]\n1158:                                 ->getListenSocket(0)\n1159:                                 ->isOpen() &&\n1160:                             listener.hasCompatibleAddress(*draining_listener.listener_);\n1161:                    });\n1162: \n1163:   if (existing_draining_listener != draining_listeners_.cend()) {\n1164:     existing_draining_listener->listener_->debugLog(\"clones listener sockets\");\n1165:     draining_listener_ptr = existing_draining_listener->listener_.get();\n1166:   } else {\n1167:     auto existing_draining_filter_chain = std::find_if(\n1168:         draining_filter_chains_manager_.cbegin(), draining_filter_chains_manager_.cend(),\n1169:         [&listener](const DrainingFilterChainsManager& draining_filter_chain) {\n1170:           return draining_filter_chain.getDrainingListener()\n1171:                      .listenSocketFactories()[0]\n1172:                      ->getListenSocket(0)\n1173:                      ->isOpen() &&\n1174:                  listener.hasCompatibleAddress(draining_filter_chain.getDrainingListener());\n1175:         });\n1176: \n1177:     if (existing_draining_filter_chain != draining_filter_chains_manager_.cend()) {\n1178:       existing_draining_filter_chain->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\"clones listener socket\");\n1179:       draining_listener_ptr = &existing_draining_filter_chain->getDrainingListener();\n1180:     }\n1181:   }\n1182: \n1183:   if (draining_listener_ptr != nullptr) {\n1184:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(listener.cloneSocketFactoryFrom(*draining_listener_ptr));\n1185:   } else {\n1186:     return createListenSocketFactory(listener);\n1187:   }\n1188:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1189: }\n1190: \n1191: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n1192:   Network::Socket::Type socket_type = listener.socketType();\n1193:   ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type = ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoBind;\n1194:   if (listener.bindToPort()) {\n1195:     bind_type = listener.reusePort() ? ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort\n1196:                                      : ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoReusePort;\n1197:   }\n1198:   absl::Status socket_status = absl::OkStatus();\n1199:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n1200:     Network::SocketCreationOptions creation_options;\n1201:     creation_options.mptcp_enabled_ = listener.mptcpEnabled();\n1202:     for (std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>::size_type i = 0;\n1203:          i < listener.addresses().size(); i++) {\n1204:       auto factory_or_error = ListenSocketFactoryImpl::create(\n1205:           *factory_, listener.addresses()[i], socket_type, listener.listenSocketOptions(i),\n1206:           listener.name(), listener.tcpBacklogSize(), bind_type, creation_options,\n1207:           server_.options().concurrency());\n1208:       if (!factory_or_error.status().ok()) {\n1209:         socket_status = factory_or_error.status();\n1210:       } else {\n1211:         socket_status = listener.addSocketFactory(std::move(*factory_or_error));\n1212:       }\n1213:       if (!socket_status.ok()) {\n1214:         break;\n1215:       }\n1216:     }\n1217:   }\n1218:   END_TRY\n1219:   CATCH(const EnvoyException& e, {\n1220:     socket_status = absl::InvalidArgumentError(e.what());\n1221:     ;\n1222:   });\n1223:   if (!socket_status.ok()) {\n1224:     ENVOY_LOG(error, \"listener '{}' failed to bind or apply socket options: {}\", listener.name(),\n1225:               socket_status.message());\n1226:     incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n1227:   }\n1228:   return socket_status;\n1229: }\n1230: \n1231: void ListenerManagerImpl::maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n1232:   if (!listener.udpListenerConfig().has_value() ||\n1233:       listener.udpListenerConfig()->listenerFactory().isTransportConnectionless()) {\n1234:     // Close the listen sockets right away to avoid leaving TCP connections in accept queue\n1235:     // already waiting for long timeout. However, connection-oriented UDP listeners shouldn't\n1236:     // close the socket because they need to receive packets for existing connections via the\n1237:     // listen sockets.\n1238:     listener.closeAllSockets();\n1239: \n1240:     // In case of this listener was in-place updated previously and in the filter chains draining\n1241:     // procedure, so close the sockets for the previous draining listener.\n1242:     for (auto& manager : draining_filter_chains_manager_) {\n1243:       // A listener can be in-place updated multiple times, so there may\n1244:       // have multiple draining listeners with same tag.\n1245:       if (manager.getDrainingListenerTag() == listener.listenerTag()) {\n1246:         manager.getDrainingListener().closeAllSockets();\n1247:       }\n1248:     }\n1249:   }\n1250: }\n1251: \n1252: ApiListenerOptRef ListenerManagerImpl::apiListener() {\n1253:   return api_listener_ ? ApiListenerOptRef(std::ref(*api_listener_)) : absl::nullopt;\n1254: }\n1255: \n1256: REGISTER_FACTORY(DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl, ListenerManagerFactory);\n1257: \n1258: } // namespace Server\n1259: } // namespace Envoy\n1260: ",

  }
