{\n46: \n47: bool anyFilterChain(\n48:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n49:     std::function<bool(const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain&)> predicate) {\n50:   return (config.has_default_filter_chain() && predicate(config.default_filter_chain())) ||\n51:          std::any_of(config.filter_chains().begin(), config.filter_chains().end(), predicate);\n52: }\n53: \n54: bool usesProxyProto(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n55:   // Checking only the first or default filter chain is done for backwards compatibility.\n56:   const bool use_proxy_proto = PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(\n57:       config.filter_chains().empty() ? config.default_filter_chain() : config.filter_chains()[0],\n58:       use_proxy_proto, false);\n59:   if (use_proxy_proto) {\n60:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn,\n61:                    \"using deprecated field 'use_proxy_proto' is dangerous as it does not respect \"\n62:                    \"listener filter order. Do not use this field and instead configure the proxy \"\n63:                    \"proto listener filter directly.\");\n64:   }\n65: \n66:   return use_proxy_proto;\n67: }\n68: \n69: bool shouldBindToPort(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n70:   return PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, bind_to_port, true) &&\n71:          PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config.deprecated_v1(), bind_to_port, true);\n72: }\n73: } // namespace\n74: \n75: absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenSocketFactoryImpl>> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::create(\n76:     ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address,\n77:     Network::Socket::Type socket_type, const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n78:     const std::string& listener_name, uint32_t tcp_backlog_size,\n79:     ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type,\n80:     const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t num_sockets) {\n81:   absl::Status creation_status = absl::OkStatus();\n82:   auto ret = std::unique_ptr<ListenSocketFactoryImpl>(new ListenSocketFactoryImpl(\n83:       factory, address, socket_type, options, listener_name, tcp_backlog_size, bind_type,\n84:       creation_options, num_sockets, creation_status));\n85:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n86:   return ret;\n87: }\n88: \n89: ListenSocketFactoryImpl::ListenSocketFactoryImpl(\n90:     ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address,\n91:     Network::Socket::Type socket_type, const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n92:     const std::string& listener_name, uint32_t tcp_backlog_size,\n93:     ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type,\n94:     const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t num_sockets,\n95:     absl::Status& creation_status)\n96:     : factory_(factory), local_address_(address), socket_type_(socket_type), options_(options),\n97:       listener_name_(listener_name), tcp_backlog_size_(tcp_backlog_size), bind_type_(bind_type),\n98:       socket_creation_options_(creation_options) {\n99: \n100:   if (local_address_->type() == Network::Address::Type::Ip) {\n101:     if (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Datagram) {\n102:       ASSERT(bind_type_ == ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort || num_sockets == 1u);\n103:     }\n104:   } else {\n105:     if (local_address_->type() == Network::Address::Type::Pipe) {\n106:       // Listeners with Unix domain socket always use shared socket.\n107:       // TODO(mattklein123): This should be blocked at the config parsing layer instead of getting\n108:       // here and disabling reuse_port.\n109:       if (bind_type_ == ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort) {\n110:         bind_type_ = ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoReusePort;\n111:       }\n112:     } else {\n113:       ASSERT(local_address_->type() == Network::Address::Type::EnvoyInternal);\n114:       bind_type_ = ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoBind;\n115:     }\n116:   }\n117: \n118:   auto socket_or_error = createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(factory, socket_type, 0);\n119:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(socket_or_error.status(), creation_status);\n120:   sockets_.push_back(*socket_or_error);\n121: \n122:   if (sockets_[0] != nullptr && local_address_->ip() && local_address_->ip()->port() == 0) {\n123:     local_address_ = sockets_[0]->connectionInfoProvider().localAddress();\n124:   }\n125:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Set listener {} socket factory local address to {}\", listener_name,\n126:             local_address_->asString());\n127: \n128:   // Now create the remainder of the sockets that will be used by the rest of the workers.\n129:   for (uint32_t i = 1; i < num_sockets; i++) {\n130:     if (bind_type_ != ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort && sockets_[0] != nullptr) {\n131:       sockets_.push_back(sockets_[0]->duplicate());\n132:     } else {\n133:       auto socket_or_error = createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(factory, socket_type, i);\n134:       SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(socket_or_error.status(), creation_status);\n135:       sockets_.push_back(*socket_or_error);\n136:     }\n137:   }\n138:   ASSERT(sockets_.size() == num_sockets);\n139: }\n140: \n141: ListenSocketFactoryImpl::ListenSocketFactoryImpl(const ListenSocketFactoryImpl& factory_to_clone)\n142:     : factory_(factory_to_clone.factory_), local_address_(factory_to_clone.local_address_),\n143:       socket_type_(factory_to_clone.socket_type_), options_(factory_to_clone.options_),\n144:       listener_name_(factory_to_clone.listener_name_),\n145:       tcp_backlog_size_(factory_to_clone.tcp_backlog_size_),\n146:       bind_type_(factory_to_clone.bind_type_),\n147:       socket_creation_options_(factory_to_clone.socket_creation_options_) {\n148:   for (auto& socket : factory_to_clone.sockets_) {\n149:     // In the cloning case we always duplicate() the socket. This makes sure that during listener\n150:     // update/drain we don't lose any incoming connections when using reuse_port. Specifically on\n151:     // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\n152:     // on the same address. Incoming connections are immediately assigned to one of these queues.\n153:     // If connections are in the queue when the socket is closed, they are closed/reset, not sent to\n154:     // another queue. So avoid making extra queues in the kernel, even temporarily.\n155:     //\n156:     // TODO(mattklein123): In the current code as long as the address matches, the socket factory\n157:     // will be cloned, effectively ignoring any changed socket options. The code should probably\n158:     // block any updates to listeners that use the same address but different socket options.\n159:     // (It's possible we could handle changing some socket options, but this would be tricky and\n160:     // probably not worth the difficulty.)\n161:     sockets_.push_back(socket->duplicate());\n162:   }\n163: }\n164: \n165: absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(\n166:     ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Socket::Type socket_type, uint32_t worker_index) {\n167:   // Socket might be nullptr when doing server validation.\n168:   // TODO(mattklein123): See the comment in the validation code. Make that code not return nullptr\n169:   // so this code can be simpler.\n170:   absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> socket_or_error = factory.createListenSocket(\n171:       local_address_, socket_type, options_, bind_type_, socket_creation_options_, worker_index);\n172:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(socket_or_error.status());\n173:   Network::SocketSharedPtr socket = std::move(*socket_or_error);\n174: \n175:   // Binding is done by now.\n176:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Create listen socket for listener {} on address {}\", listener_name_,\n177:             local_address_->asString());\n178:   if (socket != nullptr && options_ != nullptr) {\n179:     const bool ok = Network::Socket::applyOptions(\n180:         options_, *socket, envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption::STATE_BOUND);\n181:     const std::string message =\n182:         fmt::format(\"{}: Setting socket options {}\", listener_name_, ok ? \"succeeded\" : \"failed\");\n183:     if (!ok) {\n184:       ENVOY_LOG(warn, \"{}\", message);\n185:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(message);\n186:     } else {\n187:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"{}\", message);\n188:     }\n189: \n190:     // Add the options to the socket_ so that STATE_LISTENING options can be\n191:     // set after listen() is called and immediately before the workers start running.\n192:     socket->addOptions(options_);\n193:   }\n194:   return socket;\n195: }\n196: \n197: Network::SocketSharedPtr ListenSocketFactoryImpl::getListenSocket(uint32_t worker_index) {\n198:   // Per the TODO above, sockets at this point can never be null. That only happens in the\n199:   // config validation path.\n200:   ASSERT(worker_index < sockets_.size() && sockets_[worker_index] != nullptr);\n201:   return sockets_[worker_index];\n202: }\n203: \n204: absl::Status ListenSocketFactoryImpl::doFinalPreWorkerInit() {\n205:   if (bind_type_ == ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoBind ||\n206:       socket_type_ != Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n207:     return absl::OkStatus();\n208:   }\n209: \n210:   ASSERT(!sockets_.empty());\n211:   auto listen_and_apply_options = [](Envoy::Network::SocketSharedPtr socket, int tcp_backlog_size) {\n212:     const auto rc = socket->ioHandle().listen(tcp_backlog_size);\n213:     if (rc.return_value_ != 0) {\n214:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\"cannot listen() errno={}\", rc.errno_));\n215:     }\n216:     if (!Network::Socket::applyOptions(socket->options(), *socket,\n217:                                        envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption::STATE_LISTENING)) {\n218:       std::string message =\n219:           fmt::format(\"cannot set post-listen socket option on socket: {}\",\n220:                       socket->connectionInfoProvider().localAddress()->asString());\n221:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(message);\n222:     }\n223:     return absl::OkStatus();\n224:   };\n225:   // On all platforms we should listen on the first socket.\n226:   auto iterator = sockets_.begin();\n227:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(listen_and_apply_options(*iterator, tcp_backlog_size_));\n228:   ++iterator;\n229: #ifndef WIN32\n230:   // With this implementation on Windows we only accept\n231:   // connections on Worker 1 and then we use the `ExactConnectionBalancer`\n232:   // to balance these connections to all workers.\n233:   // TODO(davinci26): We should update the behavior when socket duplication\n234:   // does not cause accepts to hang in the OS.\n235:   for (; iterator != sockets_.end(); ++iterator) {\n236:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(listen_and_apply_options(*iterator, tcp_backlog_size_));\n237:   }\n238: #endif\n239:   return absl::OkStatus();\n240: }\n241: \n242: namespace {\n243: std::string listenerStatsScope(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n244:   if (!config.stat_prefix().empty()) {\n245:     return config.stat_prefix();\n246:   }\n247:   if (config.has_internal_listener()) {\n248:     return absl::StrCat(\"envoy_internal_\", config.name());\n249:   }\n250:   auto address_or_error = Network::Address::resolveProtoAddress(config.address());\n251:   if (address_or_error.status().ok()) {\n252:     return address_or_error.value()->asString();\n253:   }\n254:   // Listener creation will fail shortly when the address is used.\n255:   return absl::StrCat(\"invalid_address_listener\");\n256: }\n257: } // namespace\n258: \n259: ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl::ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl(\n260:     Envoy::Server::Instance& server, ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\n261:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config, DrainManagerPtr drain_manager)\n262:     : Server::FactoryContextImplBase(\n263:           server, validation_visitor, server.stats().createScope(\"\"),\n264:           server.stats().createScope(fmt::format(\"listener.{}.\", listenerStatsScope(config))),\n265:           std::make_shared<ListenerInfoImpl>(config)),\n266:       drain_manager_(std::move(drain_manager)) {}\n267: \n268: Network::DrainDecision& ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl::drainDecision() { return *this; }\n269: Server::DrainManager& ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl::drainManager() { return *drain_manager_; }\n270: Init::Manager& ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl::initManager() { return server_.initManager(); }\n271: \n272: absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>>\n273: ListenerImpl::create(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n274:                      const std::string& version_info, ListenerManagerImpl& parent,\n275:                      const std::string& name, bool added_via_api, bool workers_started,\n276:                      uint64_t hash) {\n277:   absl::Status creation_status = absl::OkStatus();\n278:   auto ret = std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>(new ListenerImpl(\n279:       config, version_info, parent, name, added_via_api, workers_started, hash, creation_status));\n280:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n281:   return ret;\n282: }\n283: \n284: ListenerImpl::ListenerImpl(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n285:                            const std::string& version_info, ListenerManagerImpl& parent,\n286:                            const std::string& name, bool added_via_api, bool workers_started,\n287:                            uint64_t hash, absl::Status& creation_status)\n288:     : parent_(parent),\n289:       socket_type_(config.has_internal_listener()\n290:                        ? Network::Socket::Type::Stream\n291:                        : Network::Utility::protobufAddressSocketType(config.address())),\n292:       bind_to_port_(shouldBindToPort(config)), mptcp_enabled_(config.enable_mptcp()),\n293:       hand_off_restored_destination_connections_(\n294:           PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, use_original_dst, false)),\n295:       per_connection_buffer_limit_bytes_(\n296:           PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, per_connection_buffer_limit_bytes, 1024 * 1024)),\n297:       listener_tag_(parent_.factory_->nextListenerTag()), name_(name),\n298:       added_via_api_(added_via_api), workers_started_(workers_started), hash_(hash),\n299:       tcp_backlog_size_(\n300:           PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, tcp_backlog_size, ENVOY_TCP_BACKLOG_SIZE)),\n301:       max_connections_to_accept_per_socket_event_(\n302:           PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, max_connections_to_accept_per_socket_event,\n303:                                           Network::DefaultMaxConnectionsToAcceptPerSocketEvent)),\n304:       validation_visitor_(\n305:           added_via_api_ ? parent_.server_.messageValidationContext().dynamicValidationVisitor()\n306:                          : parent_.server_.messageValidationContext().staticValidationVisitor()),\n307:       ignore_global_conn_limit_(config.ignore_global_conn_limit()),\n308:       bypass_overload_manager_(config.bypass_overload_manager()),\n309:       listener_init_target_(fmt::format(\"Listener-init-target {}\", name),\n310:                             [this]() { dynamic_init_manager_->initialize(local_init_watcher_); }),\n311:       dynamic_init_manager_(std::make_unique<Init::ManagerImpl>(\n312:           fmt::format(\"Listener-local-init-manager {} {}\", name, hash))),\n313:       config_(config), version_info_(version_info),\n314:       listener_filters_timeout_(\n315:           PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(config, listener_filters_timeout, 15000)),\n316:       continue_on_listener_filters_timeout_(config.continue_on_listener_filters_timeout()),\n317:       listener_factory_context_(std::make_shared<PerListenerFactoryContextImpl>(\n318:           parent.server_, validation_visitor_, config, *this,\n319:           parent_.factory_->createDrainManager(config.drain_type()))),\n320:       reuse_port_(getReusePortOrDefault(parent_.server_, config, socket_type_)),\n321:       cx_limit_runtime_key_(\"envoy.resource_limits.listener.\" + config.name() +\n322:                             \".connection_limit\"),\n323:       open_connections_(std::make_shared<BasicResourceLimitImpl>(\n324:           std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max(),\n325:           listener_factory_context_->serverFactoryContext().runtime(), cx_limit_runtime_key_)),\n326:       local_init_watcher_(fmt::format(\"Listener-local-init-watcher {}\", name),\n327:                           [this] {\n328:                             if (workers_started_) {\n329:                               parent_.onListenerWarmed(*this);\n330:                             } else {\n331:                               // Notify Server that this listener is\n332:                               // ready.\n333:                               listener_init_target_.ready();\n334:                             }\n335:                           }),\n336:       transport_factory_context_(\n337:           std::make_shared<Server::Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl>(\n338:               parent_.server_.serverFactoryContext(), parent_.server_.sslContextManager(),\n339:               listenerScope(), parent_.server_.clusterManager(), validation_visitor_)),\n340:       quic_stat_names_(parent_.quicStatNames()),\n341:       missing_listener_config_stats_({ALL_MISSING_LISTENER_CONFIG_STATS(\n342:           POOL_COUNTER(listener_factory_context_->listenerScope()))}) {\n343:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<\n344:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption>>>\n345:       address_opts_list;\n346:   if (config.has_internal_listener()) {\n347:     addresses_.emplace_back(\n348:         std::make_shared<Network::Address::EnvoyInternalInstance>(config.name()));\n349:     address_opts_list.emplace_back(std::ref(config.socket_options()));\n350:   } else {\n351:     // All the addresses should be same socket type, so get the first address's socket type is\n352:     // enough.\n353:     auto address_or_error = Network::Address::resolveProtoAddress(config.address());\n354:     SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(address_or_error.status(), creation_status);\n355:     auto address = std::move(address_or_error.value());\n356:     SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(checkIpv4CompatAddress(address, config.address()), creation_status);\n357:     addresses_.emplace_back(address);\n358:     address_opts_list.emplace_back(std::ref(config.socket_options()));\n359: \n360:     for (auto i = 0; i < config.additional_addresses_size(); i++) {\n361:       if (socket_type_ !=\n362:           Network::Utility::protobufAddressSocketType(config.additional_addresses(i).address())) {\n363:         creation_status = absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n364:             fmt::format(\"listener {}: has different socket type. The listener only \"\n365:                         \"support same socket type for all the addresses.\",\n366:                         name_));\n367:         return;\n368:       }\n369:       auto addresses_or_error =\n370:           Network::Address::resolveProtoAddress(config.additional_addresses(i).address());\n371:       SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addresses_or_error.status(), creation_status);\n372:       auto additional_address = std::move(addresses_or_error.value());\n373:       SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(\n374:           checkIpv4CompatAddress(address, config.additional_addresses(i).address()),\n375:           creation_status);\n376:       addresses_.emplace_back(additional_address);\n377:       if (config.additional_addresses(i).has_socket_options()) {\n378:         address_opts_list.emplace_back(\n379:             std::ref(config.additional_addresses(i).socket_options().socket_options()));\n380:       } else {\n381:         address_opts_list.emplace_back(std::ref(config.socket_options()));\n382:       }\n383:     }\n384:   }\n385: \n386:   const absl::optional<std::string> runtime_val =\n387:       listener_factory_context_->serverFactoryContext().runtime().snapshot().get(\n388:           cx_limit_runtime_key_);\n389:   if (runtime_val && runtime_val->empty()) {\n390:     ENVOY_LOG(warn,\n391:               \"Listener connection limit runtime key {} is empty. There are currently no \"\n392:               \"limitations on the number of accepted connections for listener {}.\",\n393:               cx_limit_runtime_key_, config.name());\n394:   }\n395: \n396:   filter_chain_manager_ = std::make_unique<FilterChainManagerImpl>(\n397:       addresses_, listener_factory_context_->parentFactoryContext(), initManager()),\n398: \n399:   buildAccessLog(config);\n400:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(validateConfig(), creation_status);\n401: \n402:   // buildUdpListenerFactory() must come before buildListenSocketOptions() because the UDP\n403:   // listener factory can provide additional options.\n404:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(buildUdpListenerFactory(config, parent_.server_.options().concurrency()),\n405:                            creation_status);\n406:   buildListenSocketOptions(config, address_opts_list);\n407:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(createListenerFilterFactories(config), creation_status);\n408:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(validateFilterChains(config), creation_status);\n409:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(buildFilterChains(config), creation_status);\n410:   if (socket_type_ != Network::Socket::Type::Datagram) {\n411:     buildSocketOptions(config);\n412:     buildOriginalDstListenerFilter(config);\n413:     buildProxyProtocolListenerFilter(config);\n414:     SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(buildInternalListener(config), creation_status);\n415:   }\n416:   if (!workers_started_) {\n417:     // Initialize dynamic_init_manager_ from Server's init manager if it's not initialized.\n418:     // NOTE: listener_init_target_ should be added to parent's initManager at the end of the\n419:     // listener constructor so that this listener's children entities could register their targets\n420:     // with their parent's initManager.\n421:     parent_.server_.initManager().add(listener_init_target_);\n422:   }\n423: }\n424: \n425: ListenerImpl::ListenerImpl(ListenerImpl& origin,\n426:                            const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n427:                            const std::string& version_info, ListenerManagerImpl& parent,\n428:                            const std::string& name, bool added_via_api, bool workers_started,\n429:                            uint64_t hash, absl::Status& creation_status)\n430:     : parent_(parent), addresses_(origin.addresses_), socket_type_(origin.socket_type_),\n431:       bind_to_port_(shouldBindToPort(config)), mptcp_enabled_(config.enable_mptcp()),\n432:       hand_off_restored_destination_connections_(\n433:           PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, use_original_dst, false)),\n434:       per_connection_buffer_limit_bytes_(\n435:           PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, per_connection_buffer_limit_bytes, 1024 * 1024)),\n436:       listener_tag_(origin.listener_tag_), name_(name), added_via_api_(added_via_api),\n437:       workers_started_(workers_started), hash_(hash),\n438:       tcp_backlog_size_(\n439:           PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, tcp_backlog_size, ENVOY_TCP_BACKLOG_SIZE)),\n440:       max_connections_to_accept_per_socket_event_(\n441:           PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, max_connections_to_accept_per_socket_event,\n442:                                           Network::DefaultMaxConnectionsToAcceptPerSocketEvent)),\n443:       validation_visitor_(\n444:           added_via_api_ ? parent_.server_.messageValidationContext().dynamicValidationVisitor()\n445:                          : parent_.server_.messageValidationContext().staticValidationVisitor()),\n446:       ignore_global_conn_limit_(config.ignore_global_conn_limit()),\n447:       bypass_overload_manager_(config.bypass_overload_manager()),\n448:       // listener_init_target_ is not used during in place update because we expect server started.\n449:       listener_init_target_(\"\", nullptr),\n450:       dynamic_init_manager_(std::make_unique<Init::ManagerImpl>(\n451:           fmt::format(\"Listener-local-init-manager {} {}\", name, hash))),\n452:       config_(config), version_info_(version_info),\n453:       listen_socket_options_list_(origin.listen_socket_options_list_),\n454:       listener_filters_timeout_(\n455:           PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(config, listener_filters_timeout, 15000)),\n456:       continue_on_listener_filters_timeout_(config.continue_on_listener_filters_timeout()),\n457:       udp_listener_config_(origin.udp_listener_config_),\n458:       connection_balancers_(origin.connection_balancers_),\n459:       // Reuse the listener_factory_context_base_ from the origin listener because the filter chain\n460:       // only updates will not change the listener_factory_context_base_.\n461:       listener_factory_context_(std::make_shared<PerListenerFactoryContextImpl>(\n462:           origin.listener_factory_context_->listener_factory_context_base_, *this)),\n463:       filter_chain_manager_(std::make_unique<FilterChainManagerImpl>(\n464:           addresses_, origin.listener_factory_context_->parentFactoryContext(), initManager(),\n465:           *origin.filter_chain_manager_)),\n466:       reuse_port_(origin.reuse_port_),\n467:       local_init_watcher_(fmt::format(\"Listener-local-init-watcher {}\", name),\n468:                           [this] {\n469:                             ASSERT(workers_started_);\n470:                             parent_.inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(*this);\n471:                           }),\n472:       transport_factory_context_(origin.transport_factory_context_),\n473:       quic_stat_names_(parent_.quicStatNames()),\n474:       missing_listener_config_stats_({ALL_MISSING_LISTENER_CONFIG_STATS(\n475:           POOL_COUNTER(listener_factory_context_->listenerScope()))}) {\n476:   buildAccessLog(config);\n477:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(validateConfig(), creation_status);\n478:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(createListenerFilterFactories(config), creation_status);\n479:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(validateFilterChains(config), creation_status);\n480:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(buildFilterChains(config), creation_status);\n481:   SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(buildInternalListener(config), creation_status);\n482:   if (socket_type_ == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n483:     // Apply the options below only for TCP.\n484:     buildSocketOptions(config);\n485:     buildOriginalDstListenerFilter(config);\n486:     buildProxyProtocolListenerFilter(config);\n487:     open_connections_ = origin.open_connections_;\n488:   }\n489: }\n490: \n491: absl::Status\n492: ListenerImpl::checkIpv4CompatAddress(const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& address,\n493:                                      const envoy::config::core::v3::Address& proto_address) {\n494:   if ((address->type() == Network::Address::Type::Ip &&\n495:        proto_address.socket_address().ipv4_compat()) &&\n496:       (address->ip()->version() != Network::Address::IpVersion::v6 ||\n497:        (!address->ip()->isAnyAddress() &&\n498:         address->ip()->ipv6()->v4CompatibleAddress() == nullptr))) {\n499:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n500:         \"Only IPv6 address '::' or valid IPv4-mapped IPv6 address can set ipv4_compat: {}\",\n501:         address->asStringView()));\n502: absl::Status\n609: ListenerImpl::buildUdpListenerFactory(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n610:                                       uint32_t concurrency) {\n611:   if Right now this causes\n642:     // non-QUIC integration tests to fail, which I haven't investigated yet. Additionally, from\n643:     // looking at the GSO code there are substantial copying inefficiency so I don't think it's\n644:     // wise to enable to globally for now.   "command": "python3 << 'PYTHON_EOF'\nimport urllib.request\nimport re\n\n# Fetch the file from Sourcegraph raw endpoint\nurl = \"https://sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0/-/raw/source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.cc\"\n\ntry:\n    with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as response:\n        content = response.read().decode('utf-8')\n    \n    # Replace ListenerManagerImpl with ListenerOrchestrator\n    # We specifically want to replace ListenerManagerImpl& -> ListenerOrchestrator&\n    content_updated = content.replace('ListenerManagerImpl&', 'ListenerOrchestrator&')\n    content_updated = content_updated.replace('ListenerManagerImpl ', 'ListenerOrchestrator ')\n    \n    # Write to file\n    with open('/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.cc', 'w') as f:\n        f.write(content_updated)\n    \n    print(f\"File created: {len(content_updated)} characters\")\n    print(f\"Occurrences of ListenerOrchestrator& in result: {content_updated.count('ListenerOrchestrator&')}\")\n    print(f\"Occurrences of ListenerManagerImpl in result: {content_updated.count('ListenerManagerImpl')}\")\n    \nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f\"Error: {e}\")\n    print(\"Trying alternative approach...\")\n\nPYTHON_EOF\n"
}

(usesProxyProto(config)) {\\n896:     auto& factory =\\n897:         Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactoryByName<Configuration::NamedListenerFilterConfigFactory>(\\n898: envoy::extensions::filters::listener::proxy_protocol::v3::ProxyProtocol(), nullptr,\\n902:         *listener_factory_context_);\\n903:     auto* cfg_provider_manager = parent_.factory_->getTcpListenerConfigProviderManager();\\n904:     auto filter_config_provider = cfg_provider_manager->createStaticFilterConfigProvider(\\n905:         callback, \\\"envoy.filters.listener.proxy_protocol\\\");\\n906:     listener_filter_factories_.push_back(std::move(filter_config_provider));\\n907:   }\\n908: }\\n909: PerListenerFactoryContextImpl::PerListenerFactoryContextImpl(\\n910:     Envoy::Server::Instance& server, ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\\n911:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config_message, ListenerImpl& listener_impl,\\n912:     DrainManagerPtr drain_manager)\\n913:     : listener_factory_context_base_(std::make_shared<ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl>(\\n914:           server, validation_visitor, config_message, std::move(drain_manager))),\\n915:       listener_impl_(listener_impl) {}\\n916: \\n917: Network::DrainDecision& PerListenerFactoryContextImpl::drainDecision() { PANIC(\\\"not implemented\\\"); }\\n918: \\n919: Stats::Scope& PerListenerFactoryContextImpl::scope() {\\n920:   return listener_factory_context_base_->scope();\\n921: }\\n922: \\n923: const Network::ListenerInfo& PerListenerFactoryContextImpl::listenerInfo() const {\\n924:   return listener_factory_context_base_->listenerInfo();\\n925: }\\n926: \\n927: ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor&\\n928: PerListenerFactoryContextImpl::messageValidationVisitor() const {\\n929:   return listener_factory_context_base_->messageValidationVisitor();\\n930: }\\n931: Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& PerListenerFactoryContextImpl::serverFactoryContext() const {\\n932:   return listener_factory_context_base_->serverFactoryContext();\\n933: }\\n934: Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContext&\\n935: PerListenerFactoryContextImpl::getTransportSocketFactoryContext() const {\\n936:   return listener_factory_context_base_->getTransportSocketFactoryContext();\\n937: }\\n938: Stats::Scope& PerListenerFactoryContextImpl::listenerScope() {\\n939:   return listener_factory_context_base_->listenerScope();\\n940: }\\n941: Init::Manager& PerListenerFactoryContextImpl::initManager() { return listener_impl_.initManager(); }\\n942: \\n943: bool ListenerImpl::createNetworkFilterChain(\\n944:     Network::Connection& connection, const Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList& filter_factories) {\\n945:   if (!Configuration::FilterChainUtility::buildFilterChain(connection, filter_factories)) {\\n946:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"New connection accepted while missing configuration. \\\"\\n947:                      \\\"Close socket and stop the iteration onAccept.\\\");\\n948:     missing_listener_config_stats_.network_extension_config_missing_.inc();\\n949:     return false;\\n950:   }\\n951: \\n952:   return true;\\n953: }\\n954: \\n955: bool ListenerImpl::createListenerFilterChain(Network::ListenerFilterManager& manager) {\\n956:   if (Configuration::FilterChainUtility::buildFilterChain(manager, listener_filter_factories_)) {\\n957:     return true;\\n958:   } else {\\n959:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"New connection accepted while missing configuration. \\\"\\n960:                      \\\"Close socket and stop the iteration onAccept.\\\");\\n961:     missing_listener_config_stats_.extension_config_missing_.inc();\\n962:     return false;\\n963:   }\\n964: }\\n965: \\n966: void ListenerImpl::createUdpListenerFilterChain(Network::UdpListenerFilterManager& manager,\\n967:                                                 Network::UdpReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\\n968:   Configuration::FilterChainUtility::buildUdpFilterChain(manager, callbacks,\\n969:                                                          udp_listener_filter_factories_);\\n970: }\\n971: \\n972: bool ListenerImpl::createQuicListenerFilterChain(Network::QuicListenerFilterManager& manager) {\\n973:   if (Configuration::FilterChainUtility::buildQuicFilterChain(manager,\\n974:                                                               quic_listener_filter_factories_)) {\\n975:     return true;\\n976:   }\\n977:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"New connection accepted while missing configuration. \\\"\\n978:                    \\\"Close socket and stop the iteration onAccept.\\\");\\n979:   missing_listener_config_stats_.extension_config_missing_.inc();\\n980:   return false;\\n981: }\\n982: \\n983: void ListenerImpl::debugLog(const std::string& message) {\\n984:   UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(message);\\n985:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"{}: name={}, hash={}, tag={}, address={}\\\", message, name_, hash_, listener_tag_,\\n986:             absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \\\",\\\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()));\\n987: }\\n988: \\n989: void ListenerImpl::initialize() {\\n990:   last_updated_ = listener_factory_context_->serverFactoryContext().timeSource().systemTime();\\n991:   // If workers have already started, we shift from using the global init manager to using a local\\n992:   // per listener init manager. See ~ListenerImpl() for why we gate the onListenerWarmed() call\\n993:   // by resetting the watcher.\\n994:   if (workers_started_) {\\n995:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \\\"Initialize listener {} local-init-manager.\\\", name_);\\n996:     // If workers_started_ is true, dynamic_init_manager_ should be initialized by listener\\n997:     // manager directly.\\n998:     dynamic_init_manager_->initialize(local_init_watcher_);\\n999:   }\\n1000: }\\n1001: \\n1002: ListenerImpl::~ListenerImpl() {\\n1003:   if (!workers_started_) {\\n1004:     // We need to remove the listener_init_target_ handle from parent's initManager(), to unblock\\n1005:     // parent's initManager to get ready().\\n1006:     listener_init_target_.ready();\\n1007:   }\\n1008: }\\n1009: \\n1010: Init::Manager& ListenerImpl::initManager() { return *dynamic_init_manager_; }\\n1011: \\n1012: absl::Status ListenerImpl::addSocketFactory(Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr&& socket_factory) {\\n1013:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(buildConnectionBalancer(config(), *socket_factory->localAddress()));\\n1014:   if (buildUdpListenerWorkerRouter(*socket_factory->localAddress(),\\n1015:                                    parent_.server_.options().concurrency())) {\\n1016:     parent_.server_.hotRestart().registerUdpForwardingListener(socket_factory->localAddress(),\\n1017:                                                                udp_listener_config_);\\n1018:   }\\n1019:   socket_factories_.emplace_back(std::move(socket_factory));\\n1020:   return absl::OkStatus();\\n1021: }\\n1022: \\n1023: bool ListenerImpl::supportUpdateFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& new_config,\\n1024:                                             bool worker_started) {\\n1025:   // The in place update needs the active listener in worker thread. worker_started guarantees the\\n1026:   // existence of that active listener.\\n1027:   if (!worker_started) {\\n1028:     return false;\\n1029:   }\\n1030: \\n1031:   // Full listener update currently rejects tcp listener having 0 filter chain.\\n1032:   // In place filter chain update could survive under zero filter chain but we should keep the\\n1033:   // same behavior for now. This also guards the below filter chain access.\\n1034:   if (new_config.filter_chains_size() == 0) {\\n1035:     return false;\\n1036:   }\\n1037: \\n1038:   // See buildProxyProtocolListenerFilter().\\n1039:   if (usesProxyProto(config()) ^ usesProxyProto(new_config)) {\\n1040:     return false;\\n1041:   }\\n1042: \\n1043:   if (ListenerMessageUtil::filterChainOnlyChange(config(), new_config)) {\\n1044:     // We need to calculate the reuse port's default value then ensure whether it is changed or not.\\n1045:     // Since reuse port's default value isn't the YAML bool field default value. When\\n1046:     // `enable_reuse_port` is specified, `ListenerMessageUtil::filterChainOnlyChange` use the YAML\\n1047:     // default value to do the comparison.\\n1048:     return reuse_port_ == getReusePortOrDefault(parent_.server_, new_config, socket_type_);\\n1049:   }\\n1050: \\n1051:   return false;\\n1052: }\\n1053: \\n1054: absl::StatusOr<ListenerImplPtr>\\n1055: ListenerImpl::newListenerWithFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n1056:                                          bool workers_started, uint64_t hash) {\\n1057: \\n1058:   absl::Status creation_status = absl::OkStatus();\\n1059:   // Use WrapUnique since the constructor is private.\\n1060:   auto ret = absl::WrapUnique(new ListenerImpl(*this, config, version_info_, parent_, name_,\\n1061:                                                added_via_api_,\\n1062:                                                /* new new workers started state */ workers_started,\\n1063:                                                /* use new hash */ hash, creation_status));\\n1064:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\\n1065:   return ret;\\n1066: }\\n1067: \\n1068: void ListenerImpl::diffFilterChain(const ListenerImpl& another_listener,\\n1069:                                    std::function<void(Network::DrainableFilterChain&)> callback) {\\n1070:   for (const auto& message_and_filter_chain : filter_chain_manager_->filterChainsByMessage()) {\\n1071:     if (another_listener.filter_chain_manager_->filterChainsByMessage().find(\\n1072:             message_and_filter_chain.first) ==\\n1073:         another_listener.filter_chain_manager_->filterChainsByMessage().end()) {\\n1074:       // The filter chain exists in `this` listener but not in the listener passed in.\\n1075:       callback(*message_and_filter_chain.second);\\n1076:     }\\n1077:   }\\n1078:   // Filter chain manager maintains an optional default filter chain besides the filter chains\\n1079:   // indexed by message.\\n1080:   if (auto eq = MessageUtil();\\n1081:       filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChainMessage().has_value() &&\\n1082:       (!another_listener.filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChainMessage().has_value() ||\\n1083:        !eq(*another_listener.filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChainMessage(),\\n1084:            *filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChainMessage()))) {\\n1085:     callback(*filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChain());\\n1086:   }\\n1087: }\\n1088: \\n1089: bool ListenerImpl::getReusePortOrDefault(Server::Instance& server,\\n1090:                                          const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n1091:                                          Network::Socket::Type socket_type) {\\n1092:   bool initial_reuse_port_value = [&server, &config]() {\\n1093:     // If someone set the new field, adhere to it.\\n1094:     if (config.has_enable_reuse_port()) {\\n1095:       if (config.reuse_port()) {\\n1096:         ENVOY_LOG(warn,\\n1097:                   \\\"both enable_reuse_port and reuse_port set on listener '{}', preferring \\\"\\n1098:                   \\\"enable_reuse_port.\\\",\\n1099:                   config.name());\\n1100:       }\\n1101: \\n1102:       return config.enable_reuse_port().value();\\n1103:     }\\n1104: \\n1105:     // If someone set the old field to true, adhere to it.\\n1106:     if (config.reuse_port()) {\\n1107:       return true;\\n1108:     }\\n1109: \\n1110:     // Otherwise use the server default which depends on hot restart.\\n1111:     return server.enableReusePortDefault();\\n1112:   }();\\n1113: \\n1114: #ifndef __linux__\\n1115:   if (initial_reuse_port_value && socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\\n1116:     // reuse_port is the default on Linux for TCP. On other platforms even if set it is disabled\\n1117:     // and the user is warned. For UDP it's always the default even if not effective.\\n1118:     ENVOY_LOG(warn,\\n1119:               \\\"reuse_port was configured for TCP listener '{}' and is being force disabled because \\\"\\n1120:               \\\"Envoy is not running on Linux. See the documentation for more information.\\\",\\n1121:               config.name());\\n1122:     initial_reuse_port_value = false;\\n1123:   }\\n1124: #else\\n1125:   UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(socket_type);\\n1126: #endif\\n1127: \\n1128:   return initial_reuse_port_value;\\n1129: }\\n1130: \\n1131: bool ListenerImpl::socketOptionsEqual(const ListenerImpl& other) const {\\n1132:   return ListenerMessageUtil::socketOptionsEqual(config(), other.config());\\n1133: }\\n1134: \\n1135: bool ListenerImpl::hasCompatibleAddress(const ListenerImpl& other) const {\\n1136:   if ((socket_type_ != other.socket_type_) || (addresses_.size() != other.addresses().size())) {\\n1137:     return false;\\n1138:   }\\n1139: \\n1140:   // The listener support listening on the zero port address for test. Multiple zero\\n1141:   // port addresses are also supported. For comparing two listeners with multiple\\n1142:   // zero port addresses, only need to ensure there are the same number of zero\\n1143:   // port addresses. So create a copy of other's addresses here, remove it for any\\n1144:   // finding to avoid matching same zero port address to the same one.\\n1145:   auto other_addresses = other.addresses();\\n1146:   for (auto& addr : addresses()) {\\n1147:     auto iter = std::find_if(other_addresses.begin(), other_addresses.end(),\\n1148:                              [&addr](const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& other_addr) {\\n1149:                                return *addr == *other_addr;\\n1150:                              });\\n1151:     if (iter == other_addresses.end()) {\\n1152:       return false;\\n1153:     }\\n1154:     other_addresses.erase(iter);\\n1155:   }\\n1156:   return true;\\n1157: }\\n1158: \\n1159: bool ListenerImpl::hasDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerImpl& other) const {\\n1160:   // Skip the duplicate address check if this is the case of a listener update with new socket\\n1161:   // options.\\n1162:   if ((name_ == other.name_) &&\\n1163:       !ListenerMessageUtil::socketOptionsEqual(config(), other.config())) {\\n1164:     return false;\\n1165:   }\\n1166: \\n1167:   if (socket_type_ != other.socket_type_) {\\n1168:     return false;\\n1169:   }\\n1170:   // For listeners that do not bind or listeners that do not bind to port 0 we must check to make\\n1171:   // sure we are not duplicating the address. This avoids ambiguity about which non-binding\\n1172:   // listener is used or even worse for the binding to port != 0 and reuse port case multiple\\n1173:   // different listeners receiving connections destined for the same port.\\n1174:   for (auto& other_addr : other.addresses()) {\\n1175:     if (other_addr->ip() == nullptr ||\\n1176:         (other_addr->ip() != nullptr && (other_addr->ip()->port() != 0 || !bindToPort()))) {\\n1177:       if (find_if(addresses_.begin(), addresses_.end(),\\n1178:                   [&other_addr](const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& addr) {\\n1179:                     return *other_addr == *addr;\\n1180:                   }) != addresses_.end()) {\\n1181:         return true;\\n1182:       }\\n1183:     }\\n1184:   }\\n1185:   return false;\\n1186: }\\n1187: \\n1188: absl::Status ListenerImpl::cloneSocketFactoryFrom(const ListenerImpl& other) {\\n1189:   for (auto& socket_factory : other.getSocketFactories()) {\\n1190:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addSocketFactory(socket_factory->clone()));\\n1191:   }\\n1192:   return absl::OkStatus();\\n1193: }\\n1194: \\n1195: void ListenerImpl::closeAllSockets() {\\n1196:   for (auto& socket_factory : socket_factories_) {\\n1197:     socket_factory->closeAllSockets();\\n1198:   }\\n1199: }\\n1200: \\n1201: bool ListenerMessageUtil::socketOptionsEqual(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& lhs,\\n1202:                                              const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& rhs) {\\n1203:   if ((PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(lhs, transparent, false) !=\\n1204:        PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(rhs, transparent, false)) ||\\n1205:       (PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(lhs, freebind, false) !=\\n1206:        PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(rhs, freebind, false)) ||\\n1207:       (PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(lhs, tcp_fast_open_queue_length, 0) !=\\n1208:        PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(rhs, tcp_fast_open_queue_length, 0))) {\\n1209:     return false;\\n1210:   }\\n1211: \\n1212:   bool is_equal =\\n1213:       std::equal(lhs.socket_options().begin(), lhs.socket_options().end(),\\n1214:                  rhs.socket_options().begin(), rhs.socket_options().end(),\\n1215:                  [](const ::envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption& option,\\n1216:                     const ::envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption& other_option) {\\n1217:                    return Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::Equals(option, other_option);\\n1218:                  });\\n1219:   if (!is_equal) {\\n1220:     return false;\\n1221:   }\\n1222: \\n1223:   if (lhs.additional_addresses_size() != rhs.additional_addresses_size()) {\\n1224:     return false;\\n1225:   }\\n1226:   // Assume people won't change the order of additional addresses.\\n1227:   for (auto i = 0; i < lhs.additional_addresses_size(); i++) {\\n1228:     if (lhs.additional_addresses(i).has_socket_options() !=\\n1229:         rhs.additional_addresses(i).has_socket_options()) {\\n1230:       return false;\\n1231:     }\\n1232:     if (lhs.additional_addresses(i).has_socket_options()) {\\n1233:       is_equal =\\n1234:           std::equal(lhs.additional_addresses(i).socket_options().socket_options().begin(),\\n1235:                      lhs.additional_addresses(i).socket_options().socket_options().end(),\\n1236:                      rhs.additional_addresses(i).socket_options().socket_options().begin(),\\n1237:                      rhs.additional_addresses(i).socket_options().socket_options().end(),\\n1238:                      [](const ::envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption& option,\\n1239:                         const ::envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption& other_option) {\\n1240:                        return Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::Equals(option, other_option);\\n1241:                      });\\n1242:       if (!is_equal) {\\n1243:         return false;\\n1244:       }\\n1245:     }\\n1246:   }\\n1247: \\n1248:   return true;\\n1249: }\\n1250: \\n1251: bool ListenerMessageUtil::filterChainOnlyChange(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& lhs,\\n1252:                                                 const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& rhs) {\\n1253: #if defined(ENVOY_ENABLE_FULL_PROTOS)\\n1254:   Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer differencer;\\n1255:   differencer.set_message_field_comparison(Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::EQUIVALENT);\\n1256:   differencer.set_repeated_field_comparison(Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::AS_SET);\\n1257:   differencer.IgnoreField(\\n1258:       envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::GetDescriptor()->FindFieldByName(\\\"filter_chains\\\"));\\n1259:   differencer.IgnoreField(envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::GetDescriptor()->FindFieldByName(\\n1260:       \\\"default_filter_chain\\\"));\\n1261:   differencer.IgnoreField(envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::GetDescriptor()->FindFieldByName(\\n1262:       \\\"filter_chain_matcher\\\"));\\n1263:   return differencer.Compare(lhs, rhs);\\n1264: #else\\n1265:   UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(lhs);\\n1266:   UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(rhs);\\n1267:   // Without message reflection, err on the side of reloads.\\n1268:   return false;\\n1269: #endif\\n1270: }\\n1271: \\n1272: } // namespace Server\\n1273: } // namespace Envoy\\n1274: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0@75cca99ee9fea5a6b699af6a2124ec7404fe5d13/-/blob/source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.cc%3FL700-1300\"}",

return receive buffer overflown indicator.\n703:       addListenSocketOptions(listen_socket_options_list_[i],\n704:                              Network::SocketOptionFactory::buildRxQueueOverFlowOptions());\n705:       // TODO(yugant) : Add a config option for UDP_GRO\n706:       if (Api::OsSysCallsSingleton::get().supportsUdpGro()) {\n707:         // Needed to receive gso_size option\n708:         addListenSocketOptions(listen_socket_options_list_[i],\n709:                                Network::SocketOptionFactory::buildUdpGroOptions());\n710:       }\n711:       if (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\"envoy.reloadable_features.udp_set_do_not_fragment\")) {\n712:         addListenSocketOptions(\n713:             listen_socket_options_list_[i],\n714:             Network::SocketOptionFactory::buildDoNotFragmentOptions(\n715:                 /*mapped_v6*/ addresses_[i]->ip()->version() == Network::Address::IpVersion::v6 &&\n716:                 !addresses_[i]->ip()->ipv6()->v6only()));\n717:       }\n718: \n719:       // Additional factory specific options.\n720:       ASSERT(udp_listener_config_->listener_factory_ != nullptr,\n721:              \"buildUdpListenerFactory() must run first\");\n722:       addListenSocketOptions(listen_socket_options_list_[i],\n723:                              udp_listener_config_->listener_factory_->socketOptions());\n724:     }\n725:   }\n726: }\n727: \n728: absl::Status\n729: ListenerImpl::createListenerFilterFactories(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n730:   if (!config.listener_filters().empty()) {\n731:     switch (socket_type_) {\n732:     case Network::Socket::Type::Datagram: {\n733:       if (udp_listener_config_->listener_factory_->isTransportConnectionless()) {\n734:         auto udp_listener_filter_factory_or_error =\n735:             parent_.factory_->createUdpListenerFilterFactoryList(config.listener_filters(),\n736:                                                                  *listener_factory_context_);\n737:         RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(udp_listener_filter_factory_or_error.status());\n738:         udp_listener_filter_factories_ = std::move(*udp_listener_filter_factory_or_error);\n739:       } else {\n740:         absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList>\n741:             quic_listener_filter_factory_or_error =\n742:                 parent_.factory_->createQuicListenerFilterFactoryList(config.listener_filters(),\n743:                                                                       *listener_factory_context_);\n744:         RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(quic_listener_filter_factory_or_error.status());\n745:         // This is a QUIC listener.\n746:         quic_listener_filter_factories_ = std::move(*quic_listener_filter_factory_or_error);\n747:       }\n748:       break;\n749:     }\n750:     case Network::Socket::Type::Stream:\n751:       auto listener_filter_factory_or_error = parent_.factory_->createListenerFilterFactoryList(\n752:           config.listener_filters(), *listener_factory_context_);\n753:       RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_filter_factory_or_error.status());\n754:       listener_filter_factories_ = std::move(*listener_filter_factory_or_error);\n755:       break;\n756:     }\n757:   }\n758:   return absl::OkStatus();\n759: }\n760: \n761: absl::Status\n762: ListenerImpl::validateFilterChains(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n763:   if (config.filter_chains().empty() && !config.has_default_filter_chain() &&\n764:       (socket_type_ == Network::Socket::Type::Stream ||\n765:        !udp_listener_config_->listener_factory_->isTransportConnectionless())) {\n766:     // If we got here, this is a tcp listener or connection-oriented udp listener, so ensure there\n767:     // is a filter chain specified\n768:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n769:         fmt::format(\"error adding listener '{}': no filter chains specified\",\n770:                     absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter())));\n771:   } else if (udp_listener_config_ != nullptr &&\n772:              !udp_listener_config_->listener_factory_->isTransportConnectionless()) {\n773:     // Early fail if any filter chain doesn't have transport socket configured.\n774:     if (anyFilterChain(config, [](const auto& filter_chain) {\n775:           return !filter_chain.has_transport_socket();\n776:         })) {\n777:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n778:           fmt::format(\"error adding listener '{}': no transport socket \"\n779:                       \"specified for connection oriented UDP listener\",\n780:                       absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter())));\n781:     }\n782:   } else if ((!config.filter_chains().empty() || config.has_default_filter_chain()) &&\n783:              udp_listener_config_ != nullptr &&\n784:              udp_listener_config_->listener_factory_->isTransportConnectionless()) {\n785: \n786:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n787:         fmt::format(\"error adding listener '{}': {} filter chain(s) specified for \"\n788:                     \"connection-less UDP listener.\",\n789:                     absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()),\n790:                     config.filter_chains_size()));\n791:   }\n792:   return absl::OkStatus();\n793: }\n794: \n795: absl::Status ListenerImpl::buildFilterChains(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n796:   transport_factory_context_->setInitManager(*dynamic_init_manager_);\n797:   ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder builder(*this, *transport_factory_context_);\n798:   return filter_chain_manager_->addFilterChains(\n799:       config.has_filter_chain_matcher() ? &config.filter_chain_matcher() : nullptr,\n800:       config.filter_chains(),\n801:       config.has_default_filter_chain() ? &config.default_filter_chain() : nullptr, builder,\n802:       *filter_chain_manager_);\n803: }\n804: \n805: absl::Status\n806: ListenerImpl::buildConnectionBalancer(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n807:                                       const Network::Address::Instance& address) {\n808:   auto iter = connection_balancers_.find(address.asString());\n809:   if (iter == connection_balancers_.end() && socket_type_ == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n810: #ifdef WIN32\n811:     // On Windows we use the exact connection balancer to dispatch connections\n812:     // from worker 1 to all workers. This is a perf hit but it is the only way\n813:     // to make all the workers do work.\n814:     // TODO(davinci26): We can be faster here if we create a balancer implementation\n815:     // that dispatches the connection to a random thread.\n816:     ENVOY_LOG(warn,\n817:               \"ExactBalance was forced enabled for TCP listener '{}' because \"\n818:               \"Envoy is running on Windows.\"\n819:               \"ExactBalance is used to load balance connections between workers on Windows.\",\n820:               config.name());\n821:     connection_balancers_.emplace(address.asString(),\n822:                                   std::make_shared<Network::ExactConnectionBalancerImpl>());\n823: #else\n824:     // Not in place listener update.\n825:     if (config.has_connection_balance_config()) {\n826:       switch (config.connection_balance_config().balance_type_case()) {\n827:       case envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener_ConnectionBalanceConfig::kExactBalance:\n828:         connection_balancers_.emplace(address.asString(),\n829:                                       std::make_shared<Network::ExactConnectionBalancerImpl>());\n830:         break;\n831:       case envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener_ConnectionBalanceConfig::kExtendBalance: {\n832:         const std::string connection_balance_library_type{TypeUtil::typeUrlToDescriptorFullName(\n833:             config.connection_balance_config().extend_balance().typed_config().type_url())};\n834:         auto factory =\n835:             Envoy::Registry::FactoryRegistry<Network::ConnectionBalanceFactory>::getFactoryByType(\n836:                 connection_balance_library_type);\n837:         if (factory == nullptr) {\n838:           return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n839:               fmt::format(\"Didn't find a registered implementation for type: '{}'\",\n840:                           connection_balance_library_type));\n841:         }\n842:         connection_balancers_.emplace(\n843:             address.asString(),\n844:             factory->createConnectionBalancerFromProto(\n845:                 config.connection_balance_config().extend_balance(), *listener_factory_context_));\n846:         break;\n847:       }\n848:       case envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener_ConnectionBalanceConfig::BALANCE_TYPE_NOT_SET: {\n849:         return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\"No valid balance type for connection balance\");\n850:       }\n851:       }\n852:     } else {\n853:       connection_balancers_.emplace(address.asString(),\n854:                                     std::make_shared<Network::NopConnectionBalancerImpl>());\n855:     }\n856: #endif\n857:   }\n858:   return absl::OkStatus();\n859: }\n860: \n861: void ListenerImpl::buildSocketOptions(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n862:   if (config.has_tcp_fast_open_queue_length()) {\n863:     for (std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>::size_type i = 0;\n864:          i < addresses_.size(); i++) {\n865:       addListenSocketOptions(listen_socket_options_list_[i],\n866:                              Network::SocketOptionFactory::buildTcpFastOpenOptions(\n867: *listener_factory_context_);\n882:     auto* cfg_provider_manager = parent_.factory_->getTcpListenerConfigProviderManager();\n883:     auto filter_config_provider = cfg_provider_manager->createStaticFilterConfigProvider(\n884:         callback, \"envoy.filters.listener.original_dst\");\n885:     listener_filter_factories_.push_back(std::move(filter_config_provider));\n886:   }\n887: }\n888: \n889: void ListenerImpl::buildProxyProtocolListenerFilter(\n890:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n891:   // Add proxy protocol listener filter if 'use_proxy_proto' flag is set.\n892:   // TODO(jrajahalme): This is the last listener filter on purpose. When filter chain matching\n893:   //                   is implemented, this needs to be run after the filter chain has been\n894:   //                   selected.\n895:   if (usesProxyProto(config)) {\n896:     auto& factory =\n897:         Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactoryByName<Configuration::NamedListenerFilterConfigFactory>(\n898: envoy::extensions::filters::listener::proxy_protocol::v3::ProxyProtocol(), nullptr,\n902:         *listener_factory_context_);\n903:     auto* cfg_provider_manager = \"envoy.filters.listener.proxy_protocol\");\n906:     listener_filter_factories_.push_back(std::move(filter_config_provider));\n907:   }\n908: }\n909: ListenerImpl::debugLog(const std::string& message) {\n984:   UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(message);\n985:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"{}: name={}, hash={}, tag={}, address={}\", message, name_, hash_, listener_tag_,\n986:             absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()));\n987: }\n988: \n989: void ListenerImpl::initialize() {\n990:   last_updated_ = listener_factory_context_->serverFactoryContext().timeSource().systemTime();\n991:   // If workers have already started, we shift from using the global init manager to using a local\n992:   // per listener init manager. See ~ListenerImpl() for why we gate the onListenerWarmed() call\n993:   // by resetting the watcher.\n994:   if (workers_started_) {\n995:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \"Initialize listener {} local-init-manager.\", name_);\n996:     // If workers_started_ is true, dynamic_init_manager_ should be initialized by listener\n997:     // manager directly.\n998:     dynamic_init_manager_->initialize(local_init_watcher_);\n999:   }\n1000: }\n1001: \n1002: ListenerImpl::~ListenerImpl() {\n1003:   if (!workers_started_) {\n1004:     // We need to remove the listener_init_target_ handle from parent's initManager(), to unblock\n1005:     // parent's initManager to get ready().\n1006:     listener_init_target_.ready();\n1007:   }\n1008: }\n1009: \n1010: Init::Manager& ListenerImpl::initManager() { return *dynamic_init_manager_; }\n1011: \n1012: absl::Status ListenerImpl::addSocketFactory(Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr&& socket_factory) {\n1013:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(buildConnectionBalancer(config(), *socket_factory->localAddress()));\n1014:   if (buildUdpListenerWorkerRouter(*socket_factory->localAddress(),\n1015:                                    parent_.server_.options().concurrency())) {\n1016:     parent_.server_.hotRestart().registerUdpForwardingListener(socket_factory->localAddress(),\n1017:                                                                udp_listener_config_);\n1018:   }\n1019:   socket_factories_.emplace_back(std::move(socket_factory));\n1020:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1021: }\n1022: \n1023: bool ListenerImpl::supportUpdateFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& new_config,\n1024:                                             bool worker_started) {\n1025:   // The in place update needs the active listener in worker thread. worker_started guarantees the\n1026:   // existence of that active listener.\n1027:   if (!worker_started) {\n1028:     return false;\n1029:   }\n1030: \n1031:   // Full listener update currently rejects tcp listener having 0 filter chain.\n1032:   // In place filter chain update could survive under zero filter chain but we should keep the\n1033:   // same behavior for now. This also guards the below filter chain access.\n1034:   if (new_config.filter_chains_size() == 0) {\n1035:     return false;\n1036:   }\n1037: \n1038:   // See buildProxyProtocolListenerFilter().\n1039:   if (usesProxyProto(config()) ^ usesProxyProto(new_config)) {\n1040:     return false;\n1041:   }\n1042: \n1043:   if (ListenerMessageUtil::filterChainOnlyChange(config(), new_config)) {\n1044:     // We need to calculate the reuse port's default value then ensure whether it is changed or not.\n1045:     // Since reuse port's default value isn't the YAML bool field default value. When\n1046:     // `enable_reuse_port` is specified, `ListenerMessageUtil::filterChainOnlyChange` use the YAML\n1047:     // default value to do the comparison.\n1048:     return reuse_port_ == getReusePortOrDefault(parent_.server_, new_config, socket_type_);\n1049:   }\n1050: \n1051:   return false;\n1052: }\n1053: \n1054: absl::StatusOr<ListenerImplPtr>\n1055: ListenerImpl::newListenerWithFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n1056:                                          bool workers_started, uint64_t hash) {\n1057: \n1058:   absl::Status creation_status = absl::OkStatus();\n1059:   // Use WrapUnique since the constructor is private.\n1060:   auto ret = absl::WrapUnique(new ListenerImpl(*this, config, version_info_, parent_, name_,\n1061:                                                added_via_api_,\n1062:                                                /* new new workers started state */ workers_started,\n1063:                                                /* use new hash */ hash, creation_status));\n1064:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n1065:   return ret;\n1066: }\n1067: \n1068: void ListenerImpl::diffFilterChain(const ListenerImpl& another_listener,\n1069:                                    std::function<void(Network::DrainableFilterChain&)> callback) {\n1070:   for (const auto& message_and_filter_chain : filter_chain_manager_->filterChainsByMessage()) {\n1071:     if (another_listener.filter_chain_manager_->filterChainsByMessage().find(\n1072:             message_and_filter_chain.first) ==\n1073:         another_listener.filter_chain_manager_->filterChainsByMessage().end()) {\n1074:       // The filter chain exists in `this` listener but not in the listener passed in.\n1075:       callback(*message_and_filter_chain.second);\n1076:     }\n1077:   }\n1078:   // Filter chain manager maintains an optional default filter chain besides the filter chains\n1079:   // indexed by message.\n1080:   if (auto eq = MessageUtil();\n1081:       filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChainMessage().has_value() &&\n1082:       (!another_listener.filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChainMessage().has_value() ||\n1083:        !eq(*another_listener.filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChainMessage(),\n1084:            *filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChainMessage()))) {\n1085:     callback(*filter_chain_manager_->defaultFilterChain());\n1086:   }\n1087: }\n1088: \n1089: bool ListenerImpl::getReusePortOrDefault(Server::Instance& server,\n1090:                                          const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n1091:                                          Network::Socket::Type socket_type) {\n1092:   bool initial_reuse_port_value = [&server, &config]() {\n1093:     // If someone set the new field, adhere to it.\n1094:     if (config.has_enable_reuse_port()) {\n1095:       if (config.reuse_port()) {\n1096:         ENVOY_LOG(warn,\n1097:                   \"both enable_reuse_port and reuse_port set on listener '{}', preferring \"\n1098:                   \"enable_reuse_port.\",\n1099:                   config.name());\n1100:       }\n1101: \n1102:       return config.enable_reuse_port().value();\n1103:     }\n1104: \n1105:     // If someone set the old field to true, adhere to it.\n1106:     if (config.reuse_port()) {\n1107:       return true;\n1108:     }\n1109: \n1110:     // Otherwise use the server default which depends on hot restart.\n1111:     return server.enableReusePortDefault();\n1112:   }();\n1113: \n1114: #ifndef __linux__\n1115:   if (initial_reuse_port_value && socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n1116:     // reuse_port is the default on Linux for TCP. On other platforms even if set it is disabled\n1117:     // and the user is warned. For UDP it's always the default even if not effective.\n1118:     ENVOY_LOG(warn,\n1119:               \"reuse_port was configured for TCP listener '{}' and is being force disabled because \"\n1120:               \"Envoy is not running on Linux. See the documentation for more information.\",\n1121:               config.name());\n1122:     initial_reuse_port_value = false;\n1123:   }\n1124: #else\n1125:   UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(socket_type);\n1126: #endif\n1127: \n1128:   return initial_reuse_port_value;\n1129: }\n1130: \n1131: bool ListenerImpl::socketOptionsEqual(const ListenerImpl& other) const {\n1132:   return ListenerMessageUtil::socketOptionsEqual(config(), other.config());\n1133: }\n1134: \n1135: bool ListenerImpl::hasCompatibleAddress(const ListenerImpl& other) const {\n1136:   if ((socket_type_ != other.socket_type_) || (addresses_.size() != other.addresses().size())) {\n1137:     return false;\n1138:   }\n1139: \n1140:   // The listener support listening on the zero port address for test. Multiple zero\n1141:   // port addresses are also supported. For comparing two listeners with multiple\n1142:   // zero port addresses, only need to ensure there are the same number of zero\n1143:   // port addresses. So create a copy of other's addresses here, remove it for any\n1144:   // finding to avoid matching same zero port address to the same one.\n1145:   auto other_addresses = other.addresses();\n1146:   for (auto& addr : addresses()) {\n1147:     auto iter = std::find_if(other_addresses.begin(), other_addresses.end(),\n1148:                              [&addr](const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& other_addr) {\n1149:                                return *addr == *other_addr;\n1150:                              });\n1151:     if (iter == other_addresses.end()) {\n1152:       return false;\n1153:     }\n1154:     other_addresses.erase(iter);\n1155:   }\n1156:   return true;\n1157: }\n1158: \n1159: bool ListenerImpl::hasDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerImpl& other) const {\n1160:   // Skip the duplicate address check if this is the case of a listener update with new socket\n1161:   // options.\n1162:   if ((name_ == other.name_) &&\n1163:       !ListenerMessageUtil::socketOptionsEqual(config(), other.config())) {\n1164:     return false;\n1165:   }\n1166: \n1167:   if (socket_type_ != other.socket_type_) {\n1168:     return false;\n1169:   }\n1170:   // For listeners that do not bind or listeners that do not bind to port 0 we must check to make\n1171:   // sure we are not duplicating the address. This avoids ambiguity about which non-binding\n1172:   // listener is used or even worse for the binding to port != 0 and reuse port case multiple\n1173:   // different listeners receiving connections destined for the same port.\n1174:   for (auto& other_addr : other.addresses()) {\n1175:     if (other_addr->ip() == nullptr ||\n1176:         (other_addr->ip() != nullptr && (other_addr->ip()->port() != 0 || !bindToPort()))) {\n1177:       if (find_if(addresses_.begin(), addresses_.end(),\n1178:                   [&other_addr](const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& addr) {\n1179:                     return *other_addr == *addr;\n1180:                   }) != addresses_.end()) {\n1181:         return true;\n1182:       }\n1183:     }\n1184:   }\n1185:   return false;\n1186: }\n1187: \n1188: absl::Status ListenerImpl::cloneSocketFactoryFrom(const ListenerImpl& other) {\n1189:   for (auto& socket_factory : other.getSocketFactories()) {\n1190:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addSocketFactory(socket_factory->clone()));\n1191:   }\n1192:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1193: }\n1194: \n1195: void ListenerImpl::closeAllSockets() {\n1196:   for (auto& socket_factory : socket_factories_) {\n1197:     socket_factory->closeAllSockets();\n1198:   }\n1199: }\n1200: \n1201: bool ListenerMessageUtil::socketOptionsEqual(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& lhs,\n1202:                                              const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& rhs) {\n1203:   if ((PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(lhs, transparent, false) !=\n1204:        PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(rhs, transparent, false)) ||\n1205:       (PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(lhs, freebind, false) !=\n1206:        PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(rhs, freebind, false)) ||\n1207:       (PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(lhs, tcp_fast_open_queue_length, 0) !=\n1208:        PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(rhs, tcp_fast_open_queue_length, 0))) {\n1209:     return false;\n1210:   }\n1211: \n1212:   bool is_equal =\n1213:       std::equal(lhs.socket_options().begin(), lhs.socket_options().end(),\n1214:                  rhs.socket_options().begin(), rhs.socket_options().end(),\n1215:                  [](const ::envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption& option,\n1216:                     const ::envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption& other_option) {\n1217:                    return Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::Equals(option, other_option);\n1218:                  });\n1219:   if (!is_equal) {\n1220:     return false;\n1221:   }\n1222: \n1223:   if (lhs.additional_addresses_size() != rhs.additional_addresses_size()) {\n1224:     return false;\n1225:   }\n1226:   // Assume people won't change the order of additional addresses.\n1227:   for (auto i = 0; i < lhs.additional_addresses_size(); i++) {\n1228:     if (lhs.additional_addresses(i).has_socket_options() !=\n1229:         rhs.additional_addresses(i).has_socket_options()) {\n1230:       return false;\n1231:     }\n1232:     if (lhs.additional_addresses(i).has_socket_options()) {\n1233:       is_equal =\n1234:           std::equal(lhs.additional_addresses(i).socket_options().socket_options().begin(),\n1235:                      lhs.additional_addresses(i).socket_options().socket_options().end(),\n1236:                      rhs.additional_addresses(i).socket_options().socket_options().begin(),\n1237:                      rhs.additional_addresses(i).socket_options().socket_options().end(),\n1238:                      [](const ::envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption& option,\n1239:                         const ::envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption& other_option) {\n1240:                        return Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::Equals(option, other_option);\n1241:                      });\n1242:       if (!is_equal) {\n1243:         return false;\n1244:       }\n1245:     }\n1246:   }\n1247: \n1248:   return true;\n1249: }\n1250: \n1251: bool ListenerMessageUtil::filterChainOnlyChange(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& lhs,\n1252:                                                 const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& rhs) {\n1253: #if defined(ENVOY_ENABLE_FULL_PROTOS)\n1254:   Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer differencer;\n1255:   differencer.set_message_field_comparison(Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::EQUIVALENT);\n1256:   differencer.set_repeated_field_comparison(Protobuf::util::MessageDifferencer::AS_SET);\n1257:   differencer.IgnoreField(\n1258:       envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::GetDescriptor()->FindFieldByName(\"filter_chains\"));\n1259:   differencer.IgnoreField(envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::GetDescriptor()->FindFieldByName(\n1260:       \"default_filter_chain\"));\n1261:   differencer.IgnoreField(envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::GetDescriptor()->FindFieldByName(\n1262:       \"filter_chain_matcher\"));\n1263:   return differencer.Compare(lhs, rhs);\n1264: #else\n1265:   UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(lhs);\n1266:   UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(rhs);\n1267:   // Without message reflection, err on the side of reloads.\n1268:   return false;\n1269: #endif\n1270: }\n1271: \n1272: } // namespace Server\n1273: } // namespace Envoy\n1274: ",

fetch from the file we already saved and perform replacements\n\nimport json\n\n# Read listener_impl from previously saved content\n# Actually, let me just read both chunks and combine them properly\n\ntry:\n    # Since we have the content from the MCP tool output, let's process it\n    # Read part 1 (1-700)\n    with open('/tmp/listener_impl_part1.json', 'w') as f:\n        # Store the part 1 content for later processing\n        pass\n        \n    # Let me try a different approach - Input
{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYTHON_EOF'\n# Create listener_impl.cc by combining the two chunks we fetched\n\n# Part 1: lines 1-699\npart1 = open('/tmp/listener_impl_part1_raw.txt', 'w')\n# We'll use the data from the MCP tool outputs we already have\n\n# Actually, let me read from the files I can access via the Sourcegraph API results\n# The issue is we can't easily get the full file. Let's write out a script that will\n# use sed to perform the replacement on an online copy\n\n# For now, let's manually write the file with the core replacements\n# by reading from fragments and combining them\n\nimport os\nimport subprocess\n\n# Let's try using git to fetch the file directly from the repository\n# First, initialize git repo if needed\nos.chdir('/workspace')\n\n# Try to fetch the file using git from the remote URL\ntry:\n    result = subprocess.run([\n        'git', 'remote', 'add', 'origin',\n        'https://github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0.git'\n    ], capture_output=True, text=True)\n    \n    if result.returncode == 0 or 'already exists' in result.stderr:\n        # Fetch the file using git show\n        result = subprocess.run([\n            'git', 'show', 'HEAD:source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.cc'\n        ], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)\n        \n        if result.returncode == 0:\n            content = result.stdout\n            # Replace ListenerManagerImpl with ListenerOrchestrator\n            content = content.replace('ListenerManagerImpl&', 'ListenerOrchestrator&')\n            content = content.replace('ListenerManagerImpl ', 'ListenerOrchestrator ')\n            \n            # Write the file\n            with open('source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.cc', 'w') as f:\n                f.write(content)\n            \n            print(f\"listener_impl.cc created: {len(content)} bytes\")\n            print(f\"Occurrences of ListenerOrchestrator&: {content.count('ListenerOrchestrator&')}\")\n        else:\n            print(f\"git show failed: {result.stderr}\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"git remote add failed: {result.stderr}\")\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f\"Error: {e}\")\n\nPYTHON_EOF\n"
}
